## 2026-06-20 ~22:20Z — Cycle-62 LATER-4: bank ubel #818 (head-precision × E_accept REFUTED → r is drafter-intrinsic) + reassign ubel #826 (drafter-rank diagnostic)

**Context:** #800 STILL silent (last human 19:55Z — fire stays blocked). One review-ready terminal landed (ubel #818); banked (not an official-TPS winner → official baseline stays **bi0 @218.02**). fern/lawine/wirbel flagged "worker unhealthy" by triage but all three are branch-matched to their lanes with dirty worktrees (44/292/11) = the known stale-heartbeat false alarm; no action.

### Banked (squash-merge; NOT an official winner — no BASELINE official-TPS change)
- **ubel #818 — Head-precision × E_accept: HYPOTHESIS REFUTED → r ≈ 0.397 is INTRINSIC to the MTP drafter, not a head artifact.** Squash `acb448b`, W&B group `bi0-headquant-accept` (bf16 [`94vktlsi`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/94vktlsi)/[`xtazaes2`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/xtazaes2); int4 g32 [`8g8bclka`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/8g8bclka)/[`45rxjwkk`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/45rxjwkk); g64 [`2b99mjew`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/2b99mjew); g128 [`3enknmyr`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/3enknmyr)). 4-arm head-precision sweep (bf16/int4 g32/g64/g128) on the official 128-prompt greedy decode, identical prompts+drafter. **E_accept is FLAT (~3.33) across a 0→0.10 head-rel_err sweep**; the bf16 zero-error control lands at the *bottom* (3.329), not the top → int4 quantization does NOT depress acceptance (verifier-argmax over the 262k vocab is robust to int4 rel_err ≤ 0.10). **No head-precision arm beats int4 g32 on wall TPS** (g32 246.65 > g128 243.9 > g64 241.9 ≫ bf16 211.8 = −34.8 for zero acceptance benefit). **I independently re-pulled all 6 W&B runs before banking — table accurate to <0.1% on E_accept and wall TPS.** One honest precision note: bf16 (3.3291) is *fractionally above* g64 (3.3260), so g64 — not bf16 — is the strict E_accept floor, but Δ=0.003 is noise-level (g64≡bf16 share num_accepted=46,167) and the conclusion is unchanged. **Bonus:** int4 g32 E_accept (3.370) is reliably ABOVE bf16 (3.329) by +0.041 (≈6× within-arm noise) — plausibly the QAT-matched drafter aligns marginally better with the quantized verifier-argmax. **Two consequences:** (1) head-precision is DEAD as a lever; (2) int4 g32 is CONFIRMED the correct fire-candidate head (best wall TPS + mildly acceptance-positive) → **de-risks the fire candidate** (PLE-dequant/int4head both use int4 g32 head). Methodology was benchmark-faithful (full 128-prompt, wall TPS as the honest metric, g128's steady-burst edge correctly discounted as noise that disagrees with wall TPS) — applied wirbel #814's reshuffle/wall-TPS caveat exactly right.

### ★ Synthesis update: the acceptance frontier splits cleanly into two halves; ubel #818 closes the verifier-head subquestion
The accept equation has two halves and #818 just localized the binding constraint to the SECOND one:
1. **WHICH proposals are accepted (the accept criterion)** — fully staffed: stark #816 (uniform top-k) × fern #823 (margin-gate `dp/ap`) × land #822 (AdaEDL early-stop DEPTH) × lawine #824 (ctk-down WIDTH).
2. **Whether better proposals EXIST (the drafter's proposal quality, r ≈ 0.397)** — #818 proved the verifier HEAD is not the constraint; r is drafter-intrinsic. The open question (never measured): is that drafter ceiling HARD or SOFT? → assigned to ubel #826.

### Reassigned (zero idle functional GPUs)
- **ubel #826** (`ubel/drafter-rank-diag`) — **drafter-side rank diagnostic: is r ≈ 0.397 a HARD ceiling or a SOFT/recoverable near-miss?** The mirror image of fern #820: at reject positions, measure the rank of the *verifier's argmax* within the *DRAFTER's* own logit distribution (fern measured the draft token's rank in the *verifier's* dist; nobody has measured the reverse). Build the CDF P(verifier-argmax ∈ drafter top-m) + a cross-tab by draft position. IF soft (verifier choice often drafter-rank-2/3), project the single cheapest non-tree recovery (rank-2 fallback at the FIRST reject only, honest extra-verifier-check cost) via the oracle curve. **CRITICAL scope guard: diagnostic + projection ONLY, NOT a tree/EAGLE implementation (#799 dead) — Step 2 estimates the ceiling on any drafter recovery to decide if the direction is worth re-opening; it does not authorize building anything.** Reuses fern's `reject_rank_capture` harness; LOCAL, no kernel/K/head change. `--wandb_group bi0-drafter-rank-diag`.

## 2026-06-20 ~22:05Z — Cycle-62 LATER-3: bank 3 terminals (#820/#821/#814) → acceptance frontier becomes the #1 live lever + reassign fern #823 / lawine #824 / wirbel #825

**Context:** #800 STILL no new human reply (last 19:55Z — fire stays blocked). Three review-ready terminals landed together; all three banked (none are official-TPS winners → official baseline stays **bi0 @218.02**, W&B [`s63tb03x`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/s63tb03x)). The wirbel #814 escalation watch resolved itself — it was never dark (the panel pole, exactly as flagged).

### Banked (squash-merge; NOT official winners — no BASELINE official-TPS change)
- **fern #820 — reject-rank + entropy CDF: GO for uniform top-k, FLy entropy-gating REFUTED.** Squash `7576214`, W&B [`0r80mau9`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/0r80mau9) (group `bi0-reject-rank-entropy`). Output-neutral probe (proven 3 ways: 3.380 / 3.371 / 3.379 E_accept agree) over 40,638 reject positions. **Uniform-k projects +19.9% (k=2→303) to +64.4% (k=16→416)** off the ~253 anchor (32% of rejects are verifier rank-2 near-misses, 62% rank≤5). **FLy is structurally INVERTED on Gemma:** verifier is extremely peaked (all-pos median 0.202 nats, reject-pos 1.015), so the rescuable mass sits at LOW entropy — the opposite of FLy's premise; FLy's literal θ=0.3→3.74 nats catches only 15/40,638 rejects (inert). → de-risks the team's fork: build uniform-k (stark #816), do NOT pursue an entropy-gated lane. fern's #2 follow-up (gate on dp/ap MARGIN, not entropy) → assigned to fern #823.
- **lawine #821 — FR-Spec/VOCABTRIM REFUTED at the mechanism level.** Squash `216bc2c`, W&B [`5685rzdo`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/5685rzdo). The drafter does NOT project a dense 262k head — it runs a **learned, per-token centroid-masked SPARSE head** (`use_ordered_embeddings`, `num_centroids=2048`, ctk=32 → gathers only 4096/262144 = 1.56% of vocab/step; confirmed at runtime `active_tokens=4096/262144`), a strictly superior dynamic form of what FR-Spec proposes statically → **FR-Spec effectively already deployed**. Single-stream decode is per-step HBM read-bytes-bound, so slicing the resident head saves 0.0 MiB/step; the verifier head reads ~378 MiB/accepted-token vs the draft head's ~3 MiB/step (~125×) → draft-vocab axis can't net a gain. **ctk follow-up checked against lawine's own #792** (swept ctk 32→64→128 UP: +1.5% E_accept, latency-bound drafter slows, wall TPS flat, identity broke) → raising ctk is already dead; the symmetric DOWN direction is untested → assigned to lawine #824.
- **wirbel #814 — body int4 group-size g32→g128: MARGINAL-POSITIVE but DON'T-SHIP → body group-size CLOSED.** Squash `c7aa936`, W&B g32 [`zlri6u1t`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/zlri6u1t)/g64 [`ck0mzqmy`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ck0mzqmy)/g128 [`2907fab4`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/2907fab4)/panel [`13lh527d`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/13lh527d). g128 = +2.79% LOCAL prompt-averaged wall TPS (g32 234.08 → g64 236.66 → g128 240.62, monotone, cv≤0.31%, PPL all <2.42) but: (a) below the +3–5% hypothesis; (b) **NOT orthogonal** — entangled with a per-prompt MTP acceptance lottery (single-prompt probe read −46.79% because g128 collapsed on that one prompt; accept_rate swings 0.03↔0.97 across arms); (c) erodes quality uniformly vs int4head (GPQA −2.2/MMLU −7.2/GSM8K −4.2/AIME −6.7 pts) and the **GPQA pass fails Wilson-95 (lo 0.4498 < floor 0.4712)**. All 4 floors pass on point-estimate but g128 is too risky to fire. **g32 is the quality/speed sweet spot for this drafter.** Two durable cross-cutting findings (relayed to ubel #818): verifier changes RESHUFFLE per-prompt acceptance ⇒ no single-prompt TPS probes (only ≥8-prompt, ideally 128, means are faithful); and require **Wilson-lo ≥ floor**, not just the point estimate, for a robust quality pass. Step-1 banked fact: PTQ-g32 reproduces the int4head body to 0.0086%.

### ★ Synthesis: the ACCEPTANCE FRONTIER is now the #1 live lever (+52–152% to oracle r=1.0), with a coordinated 4-lane push
- **stark #816** (uniform-k accept kernel + full quality panel) — GREENLIT by fern #820: start k=2, no free knee, climb while quality holds; keep clean uniform-k (entropy-gating dead).
- **fern #823** (margin-gate `dp/ap` near-tie) — the quality-safe refinement that separates benign near-ties from genuine errors where entropy can't; offline projection from fern's own `0r80mau9`.
- **ubel #818** (head-precision × E_accept) — does the int4 lm_head DEPRESS the surprisingly-low r≈0.397; free-win hunt.
- **land #822** (AdaEDL early-stop) — cuts wasted draft DEPTH (the #774 reject tail).

Offline from `0r80mau9`; grid k∈{2,5,16} × margin∈{0,0.5,0.8,0.95}; does a `dp/ap` margin gate keep ≥90% of uniform-k's gain while removing the genuine-error (low-margin) relaxes entropy couldn't separate? Recommend one (k, margin) to hand stark #816. `--wandb_group bi0-margin-gate-accept`. LOCAL/offline.
- **lawine #824** (`lawine/ctk-down-draftcost`) — `CENTROID_TOP_K` DOWN-sweep {32,24,16,8} to cut the 20% drafter cost (the untested symmetric half of #792's ctk-UP null; #792 shows the drafter is latency-bound + E_accept flat, which positively indicates ctk-down could net-gain). Measures drafter_gpu_ms / E_accept / wall+steady TPS (n≥3) / PPL. Cuts draft WIDTH; orthogonal to & stacks with land #822's DEPTH. Honest caveat: head gather is a small fraction of the drafter step, so the saving may be small. `--wandb_group bi0-ctk-down-draftcost`. LOCAL.
- **wirbel #825** (`wirbel/firecand-tps-faithful`) — benchmark-faithful 128-prompt TPS + acceptance-variance CI for the fire candidates (int4head + PLE-dequant #817), using wirbel's own #814 methodology. With ~2 fires left and an imminent go, confirm PLE-dequant's 265.61 robustly clears 250 (lower CI > 250) on a prefill-included 128-prompt basis BEFORE we burn a fire — or CRITICAL-flag if it's a few-prompt/prefill-excluded mirage. `--wandb_group bi0-firecand-tps-faithful`. LOCAL.

- **lawine #821** (`lawine/frspec-draft-vocab`) — **FR-Spec / VOCABTRIM frequency-pruned draft lm_head.** The drafter `google/gemma-4-E4B-it-qat-q4_0-unquantized-assistant` (PUBLIC) projects the full **262,144-token** vocab on every draft step, and with `NUM_SPECULATIVE_TOKENS=6` that draft-head GEMV fires **6×/step** (vs the verifier head once/accepted-token) → a draft-head saving is amortized 6×. Step-1 logit-MASK sweep `VOCABTRIM_KEEP∈{262144,131072,65536,32768,16384}` to find the smallest keep that retains E_accept within ~1-2% (frequency rank from a GENERAL corpus, NOT the 128 prompts); Step-2 physically SLICE the draft head at that keep + index-remap to full vocab → measure net TPS (n≥3) vs int4head 256.74; Step-3 full quality panel (verifier vocab untouched → quality ~identical by construction). The only lane touching the DRAFT head; orthogonal to int4head/PLE-dequant/body-groupsize/top-k-accept. Refs arXiv:2502.14856 / arXiv:2506.22694. `--wandb_group bi0-frspec-draft-vocab`. LOCAL only.

## 2026-06-20 18:55Z — int4head FIRE ATTEMPT (land #801): LAUNCHED → ERRORED at startup on a private-repo 401 (NOT quality) → escalated #800; land→Plan-C de-risk; lawine #815 parity-gate

- **Gate gap exposed:** `scripts/local_validation/validate_submission.py:197 enforce_launch_gate` only checks `official_gate` (PPL/completion/modalities) — it **never attempts a token-less model fetch**, so it ran GREEN in a credentialed local env that the runner does not reproduce.

- **Dispositions this cycle:** (1) **land #801 → status:wip** (issuecomment-4759621560): Track-1 stay re-fire-ready (Hub-pointed manifest re-pushed durable `997a16d`), Track-2 build + LOCALLY validate the Plan-C token-free serve path (byte-identity proof + cold-start wall-clock margin + local gate parity; NO HF job). (2) **lawine #815 CREATED** (`lawine/runner-env-parity-gate`) — a local gate that runs `serve.py` in a scrubbed token-less / fresh-`HF_HOME` runner-parity env, MUST reproduce the int4head 401 RED + the public bi0 path GREEN, so this failure class is caught before a fire burns. Both local-only.

## 2026-06-20 18:25Z — PR #807 (wirbel): W4A8 body activation-quant viability — clean NEGATIVE (−16.68% decode) → BANKED (squash-merge) for harness fix + corollaries

- **Student:** wirbel / branch `wirbel/w4a8-body-viability` → **BANKED** (squash-merge `ff6708e`; not a TPS winner — a refuted lever, but merged for a load-bearing shared-harness fix + reusable corollaries + a ready W4A8 reference submission). W&B `t2msgab0` (group `w4a8-body-viability`).
- **Hypothesis:** the body is near HBM-bandwidth-bound, so halving bf16→int8 *activation* reads (W4A16→W4A8, weights unchanged) yields a large decode-TPS gain. Newly worth testing under #784 (quality-in-band, not byte-identity).
- **Result (terminal, >100σ controlled A/B; same `w4a16-ct` checkpoint + MTP K=6, ONLY variable = `VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE`):**

| Arm | local single-stream decode TPS (mean±std, n=5) | PPL | 128/128 |
|---|---|---|---|
| **W4A16** control (bf16 acts, env unset) | **507.12 ± 0.39** | 2.0057 | ✅ |
| **W4A8** (int8 acts, env=`int8`) | **422.52 ± 0.63** | 2.0105 | ✅ |
| **Δ** | **−84.60 = −16.68%** | +0.0048 | — |

  - (TPS numbers are local single-stream `probe_tps`, NOT official a10g — the transferable result is the **Δ = −16.68%** measured identically both arms. Serves genuinely: int8-act greedy output differs from W4A16, not a silent fallback. Used the `w4a16-ct` base [bf16-tied lm_head] so the GLOBAL `VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE` toggle isolates the BODY; Δ is orthogonal to head quant → transfers to int4head, and being negative cannot be rescued by composing.)
- **VERDICT — activation-precision is a clean NEGATIVE speed lever for single-stream decode on sm_86** (refuted by physics + measurement). At M=1 (or M≈7 verify) the body is **weight-read-bound**: int8 acts save <0.6% of bytes moved (activation tensor M×K vs weight K×N; ratio ≈ 4M/N ≪ 1 at M≈1) and ADD a per-token `marlin_quant_input` kernel on every body layer with no compute payoff (decode is memory-bound → int8 tensor cores give nothing). **Do NOT re-propose body/head int8/fp8 *activation* quant for low-batch decode TPS on sm_86.**
- **★ Banked corollaries (why merged, not closed):** (1) **A W4A8 Marlin path DOES serve on Ampere sm_86** (`VLLM_MARLIN_INPUT_DTYPE=int8`, choices `int8|fp8`, `envs.py:161`; NO requant — official int4 weights serve as W4A8 via runtime per-token int8 act-quant). Rules W4A8 IN on sm_86 (unlike Cutlass/Machete sm_90 walls #781/#779) — the right tool IF a future lever becomes compute-bound (heavy prefill / high concurrency); `submissions/int4_mtp_bi0_w4a8body/` banked as the ready reference. (2) **int8 MoE-act quant is quality-safe for Gemma-4-E4B** (PPL 2.0105, no outlier collapse) — a clean speed/physics negative, not a quality negative. (3) **Harness fix** (`scripts/local_validation/harness.py`: `extra_env` `None` ⇒ unset a manifest-baked key) — load-bearing for any same-checkpoint A/B that must drop a baked env var; backward-compatible.
- **Disposition:** BANKED (squash `ff6708e`). wirbel reassigned **#814** (body int4 group-size coarsening g32→g128 — fewer SCALE-byte HBM reads at the SAME W4 bits; the bf16 g32 group-scale is ~12.5% of body weight bytes, g128 cuts it ~4× → ~9% fewer body bytes on the dominant 47.6% verify-GEMM; newly legal under #784's 5% band; orthogonal to W3/W2 [#810 bits, kernel-blocked] / 2:4 [#808] / W4A8 [activation] / channelwise-lm_head [#796]).

- **Hypothesis:** the per-token sampling step + sampled-token D2H readback run as a separate eager op *outside* the forward CUDA graph, imposing a serial `cudaStreamSynchronize` that serializes the decode loop. Widening the graph to capture greedy sampling would remove that stall (byte-exact). Sharpened by stark #806: the lever is the SYNC STALL, not the tiny sampling kernel.
- **Result (terminal, W&B `wsr5i6qb`, group `cudagraph-sampling-capture`):** **NULL — the serial sync does not exist on the int4head path.**
  - **`async_scheduling` is auto-enabled** for `gemma4_mtp` (method ∈ `EagleModelTypes`; log `Asynchronous scheduling is enabled`). It keeps sampled token ids on GPU (`prev_sampled_token_ids`) and issues the D2H readback + accepted-count correction on **dedicated side streams** (`_copy_valid_sampled_token_count`, `_bookkeeping_sync` "avoid CPU sync").
  - Profiler-independent proof: **0.0 µs DtoH on the compute stream**; 100% of the readback (128.6 µs / 82 copies) on side streams 13 & 21 (`dtoh_overlap_capable=True`); the 125 host `synchronize` calls are async event waits that resolve as no-ops. Sampling = **2.30%** of GPU-busy; eager→graphed delta ~5 µs/step.
  - **Honest CUPTI caveat:** the in-process `torch.profiler` reports 48.86% "GPU-busy share of wall" — that 51% is **instrumentation cost (2.81× wall inflation)**, NOT a serial sync. The clean unprofiled loop runs 2.556 ms/tok; the served-stack profile is **99.3% GPU-busy / 0.68% host overhead**. The off-compute-stream DtoH result is the direct, profiler-independent proof.
- **VERDICT — sampling-sync / capture-scope axis CLOSED clean null.** Widening the forward graph would remove a stall that isn't there while risking byte-exactness for a ~5 µs/step kernel delta → NO-GO. The proxy gives 20.2% for bi0 whose **REAL** private gap is the documented **4.3%** → the local proxy is **~4.7× too harsh** (E_accept-predicted gap 1−2.667/3.382 = 21.1% ≈ measured → it's the K=6 drafter's chat-distribution E_accept collapse, which int4head inherits unchanged from bi0). The lm_head quant is a **distribution-independent** weight-read reduction: **+16.9% public (218.38→255.34) / +16.4% private (174.34→202.93)**, PPL unchanged. ⇒ **int4head's real private gap ≈ bi0's 4.3% + 0.36 pp ≈ ~4.7% ≤ 5%.**
- **Net:** int4head = bi0 + a clean lm_head byte-read cut → **255.34 public TPS (clears the 250 target)**, no PPL / greedy / private-gap regression over shipped bi0. **Launch action:** because the probe came back 4× over my pre-set 5% gate, I did NOT blind-fire over my own STOP — posted FIRE-CLEAR disposition on #801 + surfaced the full decomposition + FIRE recommendation to the human on #800, HOLDING the HF launch only for their one-word go (gate-override on the #1 documented kill-risk for an external submission). On 'fire' → land launches immediately (rebuild + Hub-manifest + serve staged); on 'hold' → calibrate the proxy vs bi0's 4.3% first (land follow-up #1).

- **Hypothesis:** split the unattributed **#798 "verify non-GEMM 3.28 ms (26.4%)"** residual of the int4head conc=1 decode cycle into kernel classes (attention / ~250 RMSNorms / RoPE / KV-write / sampling) — is any of it a real fusable/launch-bound lever, or is it irreducible? Microbench-friendly (value-independent at conc=1).
- **Result (terminal, W&B `azlgrinu`, group `bi0-int4head-nongemm-attrib`):**

  | kernel class | count/step | realizable (fused) ms | %HBM-BW | share of non-GEMM |
  |---|---|---|---|---|
  | RMSNorm (all) | **302** | 4.269 | ≤1% | 58.7% |
  | attention (force-2D) | 42 | 2.379 | 3–6% | **32.7%** |
  | elementwise resid/scale | ~168 | 0.221 | 6% | 3.0% |
  | RoPE | 42 | 0.217 | 2–5% | 3.0% |
  | KV-write | 24 | 0.125 | 1–2% | 1.7% |
  | sampling (softcap+argmax) | 1 | 0.065 | 28% | 0.9% |
  | **SUM (upper bound)** | — | **7.276** | — | 100% |

  - **Lever ladder:** eager **60.0** → graphed **8.91** → fused **7.28** ms/step. Launch lever (eager→graphed) **51.1 ms** + fusion lever (graphed→fused) **1.64 ms** = **52.7 ms/step ALREADY HARVESTED** by the shipped FULL@7 CUDA-graph (ENFORCE_EAGER=0) + torch.compile (Inductor). This vLLM has NO hand-written `_C` norm/rope kernel → `forward_native` (pure PyTorch), Inductor-fused.
  - **Calibration caveat (honest):** SUM(fused)=7.28 > 3.28 ms (#798) because per-op isolation can't capture full-model cross-op fusion + synthetic paged-KV attention over-costs vs real cached-KV flash → per-class numbers are **UPPER BOUNDS**. Per-class **ranking** + the **verdict** are robust regardless.
- **VERDICT — NO non-GEMM lever exists.** The 52.7 ms/step launch+fusion lever is fully harvested; the residual is fixed-floor/count-bound tiny kernels (~302 architecturally-required RMSNorm reductions at the ~14 µs reduction floor + 42 launch-free attention). Reducing it needs fewer *kernels* (Inductor already maxed) or fewer *architectural ops* (Gemma4 sandwich + q/k/v + PLE norms are fixed). **Decode is definitively body-MLP-GEMM-bound (#798); the kernel/non-GEMM side is exhausted.**
- **Knock-ons:** (1) **closes researcher idea #2** (non-GEMM RMSNorm+RoPE+KV megafusion Triton kernel, projected +8–12%) — already harvested, DEAD. (2) **Attention = 32.7% (~1.07 ms) caps surgattn-3D's non-GEMM headroom** at a fraction of 1.07 ms (already launch-free Triton flash at 3–6% BW near its small-shape floor) — moot now (#791 quality-dead) but confirms the lever was bandwidth-thin. (3) Handed to lawine #809: sampling kernel is tiny (0.065 ms) → that lane's lever is the per-token host↔device **sync stall**, NOT the kernel GPU time (which #806's microbench can't see).
- **Disposition:** clean, well-executed terminal NEGATIVE + valuable closure. Not a TPS winner (primary metric is a diagnostic %), so merge-winner N/A → squash-merged to bank the reusable microbench + verdict. stark idle → reassigned **#810** (per-layer body-MLP precision-sensitivity map → mixed-precision viability, researcher idea #4 — the last fat lever is reading fewer body-MLP weight bytes on the bandwidth-bound wall).

| C | g32 MSE-optimal calib | 255.5 | −0.5% | **2.0044 (RAISED)** |
| D | g64 | 256.1 | −0.2% | 2.0033 |

- **Why null:** the ~41 MB scale-tensor saving is **< 2% of per-token traffic** and is **amortized across the M=7 MTP verify batch** (the lm_head fires once per accepted token, not per draft) → no measurable decode-TPS movement. **Two durable insights:** (1) **channelwise W4 (g=−1) DOES serve on Ampere** (Marlin `CompressedTensorsWNA16` accepts it — earlier "g=−1 unservable" worry RETIRED); (2) **weight-MSE ≠ logit-MSE** — arm C had the best *weight* fidelity yet *raised* PPL, because logit error is what matters, not Frobenius weight error.
- **Proof:** W&B [`kxkdixob`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/kxkdixob) (group `bi0-lmhead-bytefloor`, `analysis_only=1, fires=0`).
- **Verdict — CLOSED, lever DEAD, insights PRESERVED.** Not merged (4 dead config surfaces; no submission worth banking — none beat int4head). The lm_head byte-floor is exhausted: g32 (#788) is the quality/speed sweet spot, finer/coarser both no-op or hurt. Best arm A (channelwise) = −11% lm_head bytes at quality-equal, but **only matters if VRAM-bound — we're not** (18.4 of 20.7 GiB used). lawine reassigned to **#804** (STACK private-stability pre-flight — is surgattn-3D's +5.98% prompt-agnostic?). int4head/stack candidates unchanged.

| **STACK** = int4head + surgattn-3D | native 3D split-KV (force-2D line ABSENT, grep 0) | **272.78** | 271.83/273.20/272.37 | 2.0028 | 128/128 |

The negative branch (3D evaporates once lm_head cheap) did NOT materialize — attention is still a real marginal cost after the head GEMV is cheapened; the two levers attack orthogonal bottlenecks.

PPL flat 2.0028 across both arms — but PPL is decode-path-partially-blind, so the token_id diff is the real identity proof. *Cleaner* than surgattn-3D's isolated bf16-head signal (#785: 1.76% tok / 6.25% prompts). → PPL-gated #784 candidate, NOT a reject.

## 2026-06-20 15:10Z — PR #799 (land): Tree/EAGLE multi-candidate drafter feasibility — CLOSED RED (vLLM 0.22 spec-decode is single-linear-chain only for gemma4_mtp; ★ acceptance frontier DEFINITIVELY CLOSED: depth #774 + runtime #792 + topology #799 all dead)

- **Student:** land / branch `land/tree-eagle-drafter-feasibility`
- **Hypothesis:** Tree/EAGLE-style multi-candidate (branching) drafting could raise per-step acceptance beyond the linear-chain K=6 frontier (#774's knee) by verifying several candidate continuations in parallel — the only remaining acceptance lever after depth (#774) and runtime knob (#792) were tuned out.
- **Result: RED — not servable on vLLM 0.22 for `gemma4_mtp`.** Four independent code-level blockers (exact citations):
  - `SpeculativeConfig.num_speculative_tokens` is a scalar `int` (`vllm/config/speculative.py:80`) — no `tree_choices`/topology field exists to express a branch structure.
  - `rejection_sample()` hard-asserts `draft_token_ids.ndim == 1` (`rejection_sampler.py:413`) — verify path is linear-chain by construction.
  - Gemma4Proposer / EagleProposer / MedusaProposer all emit a linear `[batch, K]` chain; no tree-attention verify kernel exists in the wheel.
  - EAGLE is separate-weight, not the shared-KV `gemma4_mtp` head we ship — even the AMBER fork is a retrain epic, not a config flip.
- **Proof:** CPU static dry-run confirms config-validation rejects both a non-scalar `num_speculative_tokens` and a `tree_choices` kwarg. W&B [`x76anf6u`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/x76anf6u).
- **Verdict — RED, lane closed (AMBER-only-if-forked, gated behind a from-scratch tree-attention kernel + drafter retrain, far below current ROI).** ★ **STRATEGIC:** with acceptance DEPTH (#774 K-sweep null), acceptance RUNTIME (#792 CENTROID_TOP_K null), and now acceptance TOPOLOGY (#799 tree RED) all dead, **the entire speculative-acceptance frontier is closed on vLLM 0.22.** Team redirects fully to **weight-byte levers** (int4head lineage #788/#795/#797, lm_head byte-floor #796, body group-size) + the surgattn quality-gate (#791). land reassigned to **#801** (int4head fire-validity pre-flight — within-job greedy-identity + private-gap, on the critical path to the >250 submission). bi0 unchanged. Closed without merge — RED feasibility, lower-value than banking; the four citations + dry-run are preserved in this log + PR #799.

## 2026-06-20 14:52Z — PR #794 (stark): Byte-identical split-KV — CLOSED (clean null: the surgattn +5.35% IS the parallel KV-reduction reassociation; no precision/order knob keeps the speedup; → wirbel #791 sole decider)

- **Student:** stark / branch `stark/bi0-surgattn-attrib-combine`
- **Hypothesis:** The surgattn-OFF 3D split-KV path is ~+5–6.69% faster on M=1 (wirbel #785); its ~3-4/128 greedy divergence from the 2D force-2D control might be recoverable to byte-identity via (a) forcing an fp32 accumulator or (b) a deterministic cross-segment combine order — shipping the speedup quality-free.

| metric | 2D control (surgattn ON) | 3D (surgattn OFF) | delta |
|---|---|---|---|
| **decode-wall TPS** (128×512, official-comparable) | **218.30** (≈ bi0 official 218.02 ✓) | **229.97** | **+5.35%** |
| probe TPS (256-tok single stream) | 506.17 (CV 0.14%) | 512.90 (CV 0.04%) | +1.33% (Δ=9.0σ) |
| PPL (61,797 tok) | 2.0055 | 2.0055 | **bit-identical** |
| completed | 128/128 | 128/128 | — |
| greedy vs 2D control | — | **124/128 identical, 4 divergent** | onsets [373,382,472,509] (73–99% of 512) |

- **Primary:** decode_wall_tps_3d = 229.97. **Test:** greedy_divergent_prompts_3d_vs_2d_of_128 = 4. W&B [`kcojzw20`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/kcojzw20) (group `bi0-surgattn-attrib`). Toggle `VLLM_SURGATTN=1` (force-2D) vs `VLLM_SURGATTN=0` (3D) on shipped `submissions/int4_mtp_bi0_surgattn`.
- **Both recovery levers structurally closed at the source:** (a) **fp32 accumulator = no-op** — `triton_attn.py:185-204` already allocates all three split-KV scratch buffers (`softmax_segm_{output,max,expsum}`) as fp32; only narrowing cast is the final fp32→bf16 store, *identical to 2D*. A forced-bf16-partials control diverges MORE in 8/8 microbench configs → fp32 is already optimal, no precision knob exists. (b) **deterministic-combine = removes the win** — serializing the cross-segment combine to reproduce the 2D order un-splits the KV dim, killing the occupancy recovery (the 1.38×–9.13× M=1 kernel speedup) that IS the +5.35%. You cannot parallelize a reduction without re-associating it.
- **Divergence = pure reduction reassociation, fp32-epsilon:** even comparing in fp32 before any bf16 cast, 2D vs 3D differ in ≥99.9% of elements in 7/8 configs, max abs only 2.63e-5. Two inherent sources: cross-segment combine order `((a+b)+c)+d` vs `(a+b)+(c+d)`, and `TILE_SIZE_DECODE`=16 (3D) vs `TILE_SIZE_PREFILL`=32 (2D). **Not a quality regression:** 3D is as close to (or closer than) the SDPA reference than 2D in 6/8 configs.
- **The 4/128 is the config effect, not noise (determinism control):** two extra serve sessions, all 6 pairwise greedy-compares — same-config run-to-run (2d_a-vs-2d_b, 3d_a-vs-3d_b) = **0 divergent**; every 2D-vs-3D pairing = **4**, identical onsets. Noise floor is literally 0, so the reproducible 4/128 is NOT byte-identical and NOT tie-tolerant-equivalent to bi0's zero-divergence band.
- **★ Methodology landmine flagged (constraint, logged to research state):** running the *official* `greedy_gate` against the cached LOCAL spec-off reference (`research/greedy_reference/…int4_mtp_bi0_surgattn…`, Jun-19) reads ~99/128 DIVERGENT for the **shipped bi0 itself** (2d:99, 3d:100, identical patterns) — a stale/cross-job spec-off reference, NOT a bi0 bug (bi0 passes the official *within-job* gate). Anyone gating a bi0-derived submission via `greedy_gate --model-id` must regenerate the reference WITHIN-JOB first.
- **Verdict — clean null; byte-identical route for surgattn-3D is DEAD.** Reconciles with land #793 (same phenomenon, caller-gate angle): surgattn-3D's +5–6.69% is intrinsically non-byte-identical. **Under #784 byte-identity is not sacred** → the lever ships *only* via a quality gate, which is now **wirbel #791's** sole decision. stark reassigned to **#798** (post-int4head decode re-profile — find the new bottleneck after lm_head dropped to 0.378 GB/tok). bi0 unchanged.

Acknowledged land's wandb-shadow fix. bi0 unchanged.

- **Hypothesis:** Raising `NUM_SPECULATIVE_TOKENS` above bi0's K=6 (to 8/10) raises accepted-tokens/step → more TPS on a bandwidth-bound decode (PR predicted K=8/10 adds 10–30 TPS).

|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| 0 (spec off) | 83.70 | 0.17 | — | — | 2.00552 | strict-safe (ref) |
| 2 | 175.52 | 0.33 | 2.314 | 0.657 | 2.00552 | candidate |
| 4 | 211.04 | 0.65 | 2.989 | 0.497 | 2.00566 | candidate |
| **6 (bi0 default)** | **220.47** | 0.51 | 3.344 | 0.391 | 2.00566 | candidate ← **PEAK** |
| 8 | 216.11 | 0.56 | 3.531 | 0.316 | 2.00566 | candidate |

- **Primary:** output_tps (peaks 220.47 @ K=6). **Test:** PPL 2.0057. W&B (group `bi0-k-sweep`): k0 `e8lhdwkw` / k2 `ud1iunbm` / k4 `r2j5hfvn` / k6 `az782ipg` / k8 `0m9u5ci4` (+ rollup `62cpnzk3`).
- **Knee is K=6:** marginal TPS/+2K = +91.82 / +35.52 / +9.43 / **−4.36** — crosses zero between 6 and 8. The K6→8 regression (−4.36, −2.0%) is ~4× the 0.5% CV (K=8 reps tight [214.0,216.1,216.1]) → a real turnover, not noise. Spec speedup at K=6 = **2.63×** over spec-off AR. **K not run beyond 8** (monotone acceptance decline + K=8 turnover already prove K≥7 non-improving; per #784 cap the lane).
- **Why K>6 doesn't pay:** per-token draft acceptance falls monotonically 0.657 → 0.497 → 0.391 → **0.316** — by horizon 7–8 the current MTP head is accepted only ~31.6%; the marginal +0.19 E[accept] from K6→K8 doesn't repay the wider verify pass (K+1 tokens) + extra draft compute. Classic spec-decode acceptance-vs-overhead knee.
- **Quality:** PPL flat ~2.0056 across every K (spread < 2e-4, 17% under the 2.42 bar). No spec-on arm byte-identical to the K=0 ref — EXPECTED on bi0's non-BI substrate (`VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=0` + int4 Marlin not batch-invariant across AR-vs-verify); per #784 these are candidates, not rejects. Divergence traced to a **rep0 cold-warmup transient** (rep1 ≡ rep2 steady-state deterministic; the median-of-3 excludes the cold rep0) — not chaotic non-determinism.
- **Harness cross-validated:** fern's local K=6 = 220.47 within 1.1% of official 218.02 (`s63tb03x`); K=6 PPL 2.00566 matches 2.0058 → the `bi0_k_sweep.py` harness reproduces bi0's headline → sweep deltas trustworthy. Banked for future spec-decode work.
- **Verdict — clean null, K=6 confirmed optimal; ★ STRATEGIC consolidation.** With #774 (acceptance DEPTH) + #792 (acceptance RUNTIME knob) both clean nulls, the **entire drafter-acceptance lever family is now tuned-out on the CURRENT MTP head** — neither deeper K nor a wider candidate pool moves TPS. Further acceptance headroom requires a **better drafter** (higher per-position acceptance at horizon ≥6 — a deeper/retrained MTP head or EAGLE-style tree drafting), a heavier lift than a config sweep. fern reassigned to **#797** (int4head × surgattn-3D STACK measurement — do the two biggest levers compound?). bi0 K=6 unchanged.

- **Student:** ubel / branch `ubel/bi0-lmhead-bytes`
- **Hypothesis:** The lm_head bf16 read (1.342 GB/token) is the only un-amortized per-accepted-token bandwidth cost not already reduced by the int4 body. Quantizing it to int8 (channelwise) or int4 W4A16 g32 should reduce per-token bandwidth and flow directly to decode TPS, since MTP does NOT amortize the lm_head (it fires once per accepted token, not per draft row).

| arm | lm_head kernel | bytes/tok | local decode TPS | Δ vs control | GEMV ms/tok | PPL | GSM8K greedy (n=200) | 128/128 |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **bf16 control (bi0)** | cuBLAS gemv2T | 1.342 GB | 219.34 | — | 2.777 ms | 2.0057 | 0.9200 | ✅ |
| Arm A — int8 W8A16 ch | AllSpark (Ampere) | 0.671 GB (2.00×) | **241.09 (+9.9%)** | +9.9% | 1.430 ms | 2.0051 | 0.9300 (+1.09%) | ✅ |
| Arm B — int4 W4A16 g32 | Marlin (same as body) | 0.378 GB (3.56×) | **256.74 (+17.0%)** | +17.0% | 0.750 ms | 2.0029 | 0.9150 (−0.54%) | ✅ |

- **Primary metric (int4 g32):** local_decode_tps_int4_head = **256.74**. **Test metric:** gsm8k_greedy_acc_int4_head = 0.915. W&B [`9tcygwjf`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/9tcygwjf), group `bi0-lmhead-bytes`.
- **Mechanism confirmed bandwidth-bound:** speedups 1.0× / 1.94× / 3.70× track byte-ratios to within a few % (~470–500 GB/s effective HBM). bf16 control reproduces #781's 2.776 ms/tok to 3 decimals. int4 isolated by `_C::marlin_gemm` op-count rising exactly +256 (once per generated token) vs the byte-identical int8 body.
- **Quality (int4 g32):** GSM8K paired: 182 both-correct, 15 neither, 2 bf16-only, 1 int4-only — statistical tie; all arms ≥ 0.867 base. PPL 2.0029 — even lower than bi0. Greedy divergence high (0.977 frac_diverged) but quality-neutral: expected cascade amplification of tiny argmax-margin perturbations in a 512-token rollout; both arms sit comfortably above all Morgan floors on this greedy subset. (Per #784: byte-identity not required; quality gate = panel.)
- **Verdict — MERGED (BEST-LOCAL CANDIDATE):** Both arms clear every validity gate under #784's loosened quality-in-5%-band gate (PPL ≤ 2.42 ✅, 128/128 ✅, GSM8K within 5% ✅, all-4-modalities ✅, TPS > 218.02 ✅). int4 g32 is the promoted winner: biggest single measured lever, +17% standalone projects ~255 official (potentially breaks 250 in one lever). int8 banked as fallback (+9.9%, lower reconstruction error 0.010 vs int4 0.067). **Full fire-prep panel (MMLU-Pro/AIME/GPQA-Diamond) assigned to ubel** (gating the HF approval issue per #784 fire-gate). Official bi0 TPS baseline stays 218.02 until HF-confirmed.

Force-2D design vindicated. (2) **The +6.69% is real-ish, acceptance-neutral, prompt-agnostic — BUT n=1/not noise-certified, EXCEEDS the ~2.6% attn ceiling, and PPL is BLIND to its quality** (prompt_logprobs never exercise the divergent M=1 decode path → both arms 2.0057). Per #784 (byte-identity not the gate; 5%-band is), NOT closed as dead → **re-gated as wirbel #791** (full MMLU-Pro/GSM8K/AIME/GPQA panel on the 3D outputs; quality-first kill-gate). If in-band → fire-worthy +6.69% candidate.

## 2026-06-20 11:55Z — PR #781 (ubel): int4 Marlin W4A16 GEMM kernel swap — CLOSED (terminal negative; kernel-swap lever does NOT exist on sm_86; the 58% M=1 BW is a spec-amortized red herring)

- **Student:** ubel / branch `ubel/bi0-marlin-gemm`
- **Hypothesis:** A numerics-equivalent faster W4A16 GEMM kernel/config (the ~92%-of-decode-step stage) can be swapped in to beat bi0's 218.02 TPS.

| metric | value | note |
|---|---|---|
| control decode TPS | **218.49** | reproduces anchor 218.02 ✓ |
| control PPL | **2.005655** | ✓ (vs 2.0058) |
| completed | 128/128 | ✓ |
| weight-GEMM share | **91.4%** | re-confirms ~92% |
| int4 Marlin body @ M=1 | 348 GB/s = **58.0%** of 600 GB/s peak | dequant + small-M tile underutil |
| bf16 lm_head GEMV @ M=1 | 483.5 GB/s = **80.6%** of peak | no dequant |
| fire-worthy variant | **0** | none constructible |

- **Primary:** decode_tps 218.49 (= anchor, no improvement). **Test:** PPL 2.005655. W&B [`uaq6btet`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/uaq6btet) (group `bi0-marlin-gemm`).
- **Verdict — kernel lever PERMANENTLY CLOSED on sm_86:** Marlin is the SOLE servable W4A16 kernel for the compressed-tensors int4 checkpoint (Cutlass/Machete need sm_90; AllSpark W8-only; Conch not installed; Exllama rejects bf16). Its one behavioral toggle (`VLLM_MARLIN_USE_ATOMIC_ADD`) is hardware-gated OFF (Ampere lacks bf16 atomicAdd). No greedy-safe tile/thread/split knobs (compile-time constants). CLI hard-rejects any `--quantization` method mismatch vs the checkpoint's `compressed-tensors`, so it cannot force a different kernel. Code-traced + empirically probed → conclusive.
- **★ KEY INSIGHT — the 58% M=1 number is a RED HERRING for this lever:** deployed serving runs the verify GEMM at **M=7** (MTP K=6 → 1 bonus + 6 draft rows), where Marlin approaches the one-wave HBM wall (~79% at M=8); the M=1 dequant/tile slack is **already harvested by speculation** (13.29 ms single-stream → 218.49 tok/s deployed = ~2.9×). There is NO idle body-GEMM bandwidth a kernel swap could claim.
- **★ Where the programme goes — the ONE untouched bandwidth lever:** the **bf16 lm_head GEMV** (1.342 GB/token, ~31% of the M=1 weight read) runs once per ACCEPTED token and is **NOT amortized by speculation** (unlike the body). Quantizing it (int8→int4) is the cleanest fewer-weight-bytes win. Caveat (ubel/memory): lm_head quant historically risks argmax instability + vocab-prune certs fired 0% — any arm carries its own quality proof (but per #784 byte-identity is no longer the hard gate → quality-in-5%-band suffices). → **reassigned ubel to #788 (lm_head fewer-weight-bytes).** Drafter-acceptance (fern #774 / land #782 / denken #783) is the compounding other axis.
- **Why closed:** original kernel-swap hypothesis REFUTED (no servable alternative kernel exists); control = anchor (no improvement). Terminal negative. Also folded in #777 cleanup (stray untracked fp8kv dir deleted; tree clean). W&B: [`uaq6btet`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/uaq6btet).

|---|---|---|---|

Separates the EAGER byte-exact result (attention locus IS sufficient) from the served path (attention locus is NOT sufficient without BI=1 for the rest).
- **Why closed:** TPS 211.42 < 218.02 (−3.3%) AND the strict-identity hypothesis is REFUTED in the production served path. ## 2026-06-20 11:30Z — PR #779 (stark): bi0 FlashInfer decode-backend screening — CLOSED (terminal negative, permanent hardware incompatibility)

- **Student:** stark / branch `stark/bi0-flashinfer-backend`
- **Hypothesis:** FlashInfer attention backend (via `VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND=FLASHINFER`) might yield faster single-stream decode on A10G vs bi0's current TRITON_ATTN 2D-force path, at identical greedy outputs.

| metric | value | verdict |
|---|---|---|
| FlashInfer compat | **CRASH at warmup** | INCOMPATIBLE |
| Control TPS (TRITON_ATTN 2D, local) | 205.38 | vs 218.02 official (co-tenant-constrained) |
| Control PPL | 1.9929 | ✓ |
| W&B | `6i58gvjk` | group `bi0-flashinfer` |

- **Root cause:** Gemma4-E4B's global-attention head_dim=512 exceeds FlashInfer's supported range on sm86 — crashes at warmup with `BatchPrefillWithPagedKVCacheDispatched: NUM_MMA_D_QK=32 → head_dim=512, no valid kernel template`. Fails in spec-off mode AND spec-on (K=0 + K=6). This is the same reason vLLM force-pins TRITON_ATTN in `config.py` for Gemma4. The `VLLM_ATTENTION_BACKEND` env var is a dead variable in this build (ignored; force-pin fires from config.py).
- **Clarification banked:** surgattn patches TRITON_ATTN (NOT FlashAttention-2) — it forces the **2D single-pass branch** of Triton's unified-attention kernel (bypassing 3D split-KV), not FA2. This was previously unclear.
- **co-tenant note:** stark's A10G has a persistent ~19.6 GiB co-tenant, giving only brief free windows for full bi0 loads. Local control TPS 205.38 (vs 218.02 official) reflects the constrained environment.
- **Conclusion:** FlashInfer attention is permanently dead for Gemma4-E4B on any sm86 GPU. The architectural head_dim limit will not be resolved in vLLM 0.22.0 or flashinfer 0.6.11.post2. - **Conclusion:** bi0 quality panel is now COMPLETE on all 4 Morgan axes (MMLU-Pro 0.644 / GSM8K 0.867 / AIME 10/30 / GPQA 0.4970) — all PASS the ≤10% degradation bar. ## 2026-06-20 10:50Z — Cycle 60 dispositions: #777 + #772 CLOSED (two private-stable dead-ends confirmed); #771/#774 redirected; #780 outage RETRACTED

- **#777 (ubel) — fp8-KV — CLOSED (negative, hardware-blocked).** Terminal SENPAI-RESULT (`terminal:true`, run `s63tb03x` = bf16 fallback == bi0, zero fp8 delta). fp8-KV is **untestable on A10G sm_86** — a serve-time hardware wall at three independent layers: **L1** vLLM init guard (`CompressedTensorsKVCacheMethod` mis-fires on a W4A16 ckpt with no `kv_cache_scheme` — ubel shipped a correct narrow relaxation that clears it); **L2** attn-forward `assert kv_cache_dtype in {fp8,fp8_e4m3,nvfp4}` rejects `fp8_e5m2`; **L3** Triton sm_86 cannot compile `fp8e4nv` (only `fp8e4b15`/`fp8e5`). Root cause: the two fp8 dtypes Triton emits on sm_86 are exactly the two vLLM's attn refuses; the two it accepts need Ada/Hopper/Blackwell. Re-confirms BASELINE.md dead-end (wirbel #141) + adds the mechanism. `int8_per_token_head` declined (separate path, likely also no sm_86 kernel, lower-EV). Cleanup of `submissions/int4_mtp_bi0_fp8kv/` folded into ubel's next card. Evidence `research/validity/bi0_fp8kv/findings.json`.
- **#772 (land) — osoi5-quality-gate — CLOSED (negative, weights-level collapse, established).** land's Option A accepted: the question is already answered by committed in-scope evidence — no GPU re-derivation needed. osoi5-baked fast path collapses quality at the **weights** level (not the fa2sw flags):

  | model | MMLU-Pro | GPQA-Diamond | AIME | source |
  |---|---|---|---|---|
  | plain int4 bi0 | 0.644 | — | 10/30 | land #762 |
  | int4 base | 0.632 | 0.444/0.470 | 11/30 | #762 / ubel #511 / dixie #483 |
  | **osoi5-12k** | **0.274** | **0.232** | **0.033** | ubel #511 `pfu3vy7c`, stark #536 `j3gjxxts` |

  Attribution settled: ubel #511 (`pfu3vy7c`) measured osoi5 through a vanilla TRITON_ATTN/no-spec/no-fa2sw serve → still 0.274/0.232 (collapse = weights); stark #536 (`j3gjxxts`) full-262k-head transplant left AIME 0.033 → `collapse_locus=BODY`. Options B/C declined (B refines body-vs-head for MCQ but doesn't change CLOSED verdict; C is moot — fa2sw_precache greedy-quality byte-identical to 0.274/0.232 AND independently DQ'd as a mirage). Corroborates the board answer to Morgan's 300+ quality question (osoi5 = the quality-collapse route; precache = the verification-invalid route — both disqualified).
- **#771 (kanna) — loopgraph+sparse-argmax — REDIRECTED (in flight, no GPU yet).** kanna's pre-impl grep (3 locations) found the 4 env flags (`LOOPGRAPH_REQUIRE_CAPTURE`, `FUSED_SPARSE_ARGMAX*`, `ONEGRAPH`) are **inert on bi0** — they're read only by `fa2sw_precache_kenyan/sitecustomize.py`, which bi0 lacks → step-2-as-written = false null. Real form is a ~400–600-line port of the loopgraph capture + fused-argmax kernels (precache + unrelated patches stripped). Approved her Option 1 with a **capture-feasibility-first gate** (cheap incompatibility smoke before the full port) + confirmed target stays `…w4a16-ct` (not `q4_0`). Honest expected gain +2–4% (~+4–9 TPS); a clean null is a valid private-stable finding. LOCAL only.
- **#774 (fern) — K-sweep — BLOCKED on infra (in flight).** fern's A10G has a leaked ~20.4 GB CUDA context (orphaned `EngineCore` from the prior `fire_bi_tax_750` session, reparented outside fern's PID namespace — unkillable from container). Kept open; fern building/validating `bi0_k_sweep.py` (K∈{0,2,4,6,8}, cross-K greedy-identity, `/metrics` accept-rate, per-K PPL) under `--dry-run`/`py_compile` only. Reap escalated to operator via #780.
- **#780 outage RETRACTED.** Last cycle's "7/8 dark" was a premature W&B-run-absence escalation (the dark-pod-discipline trap). ≥5 students did substantive work today (kanna grep / land analysis / fern diagnosis / stark flashinfer / ubel fp8 mechanism); heartbeat file stale as usual. ## 2026-06-19 ~16:58Z — Cycle-58DP: **RE-FIRE UN-BLOCKED & AUTHORIZED — denken root-caused the 58DO ERROR to a harness web-layer bug + found a submission-side runner-honored dodge that re-passes the gate byte-identically; fire-GO posted (prove-then-fire, one continuous sequence). Four banks (#747/#744/#726/#713), four idle students reseated onto a fire-support portfolio (#750/#751/#752/#753), stark #746 dark-flagged. Zero idle.**

- **THE RE-FIRE — root-caused, dodged, authorized, in flight.** denken #742 proved the 58DO remote ERROR is **100% harness web-layer, NOT our submission**: a fresh runner dep-resolve floats `fastapi`/`starlette` forward → `include_router()` appends a pathless `_IncludedRouter` to `app.routes` → prometheus `routing._get_route_name` does `route.path` → `AttributeError` → `/v1/models` HTTP 500 every poll → harness aborts before our model serves a token (our patch only touches `gpu_model_runner`). **Fix is submission-side + runner-honored** (`hf_bucket_single_job.py:397` builds `/tmp/server-venv` from `manifest["dependencies"]` via `uv pip install`): **(a)** manifest pin `fastapi>=0.115,<0.116` (the repo's OWN `harness.py:ensure_serving_http_compat` auto-pin promoted to the manifest) + **(b)** a version-agnostic `sitecustomize` guard wrapping `_get_route_name`→None on `AttributeError`. Official-gate **re-passes BYTE-IDENTICALLY** — PPL **2.0055150113084133**, 128/128, all four modalities — dodge changes only the web stack, not the model. W&B: dodge [`9lxinip8`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/9lxinip8), gate re-pass [`vib85ldt`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/vib85ldt).
- **Fire-GO authorization** posted to denken #742 ([issuecomment-4753354758](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/pull/742#issuecomment-4753354758)) — pre-authorized, ONE continuous local sequence, no advisor round-trip: apply (a)+(b) → run the **cheap GPU-local end-to-end served proof** (unpatched `/v1/models` **500** → dodge-applied **200** through the real api_server, no HF slot — closes the one honest gap: the LOCAL gate masks the 500 because the harness auto-repairs fastapi before serving) → re-run `validate_submission --official-gate` PASS → **FIRE ONCE** (`train.py --launch --wait`, paste the full launch log + `/v1/jobs:run` line + job_id + official tps from `summary.json`). Guards: fire exactly once; submission-dir changes limited to the (a)+(b) infra dodge (no model/engine/speed/quality touch; G1 reproduction-risk unchanged); if it ERRORs on a *different* infra cause, HOLD — no blind third fire. Human un-blocked update posted to #730 ([issuecomment-4753358635](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/issues/730#issuecomment-4753358635)). **The instant the official TPS lands → HF board post tagging [@human-cmpatino](https://huggingface.co/cmpatino) for verification (per #730).** Rationale: we already burned one slot on the errored fire; this one goes into a runner we've *proven* will accept it, not blind.
- **wirbel #747 (BANK, squash-merged, commit 270b722) — `BI_VERIFY_ALREADY_BYTE_EXACT`.** Under `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1` BOTH decode (q=1) AND verify (q=K) take the 2D single-pass attention path → no reduction-order mismatch → **batched M=K verify is ALREADY byte-exact, NO route-b kernel needed** (`max_bitdiff=0`). The #680/FACT-2 896-bit divergence is a **BI=0 phenomenon ONLY** ⇒ the #730 fire (BI=1) very likely carries **ZERO token-divergence DQ** and needs no extra verify kernel — reframes stark #746's expensive K-sequential M=1 route-b away from the kernel. W&B [`wyzhvplb`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/wyzhvplb) + [`3p8jls2a`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/3p8jls2a). Analysis-only; no baseline change.
- **lawine #744 (BANK, squash-merged, commit 7fe09bd) — `PUBLISHABLE_CEILING_INTRINSIC`.** Publishable (stock-Hub drafter) K=4 spec: `official_equiv_anchored=235.69`, wall 197.73, PPL 2.0189, `official_tps=0` (anchored estimate, never run on the official basis); the publishable-drafter acceptance penalty is intrinsic. Hardens the safer high-TPS, **G1-immune** (public drafter → reproducible) submission ceiling. W&B [`iem8qznk`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/iem8qznk). Analysis-only; no baseline change.
- **ubel #726 (BANK, squash-merged, commit c3f65d5) — `INT8_LOCUS_EDGE_ARTIFACT`.** On a provenance-clean substrate the int8-locus AIME edge over full-g32 vanishes: `int8_locus_minus_g32_clean=-0.02`, `edge_replicates=0` → the apparent edge was an artifact; **full-g32 is the pick**, mooting the int8 mandate-break as a quality lever. W&B [`duuxcuic`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/duuxcuic). Analysis-only; no baseline change.
- **fern #713 (BANK, squash-merged, commit 9c9249e) — `G32_LOCUS_SHORT`.** Within-mandate g32-on-L14-27 gives a **statistically significant** int4-body AIME recovery: paired McNemar **+0.060** (exact p=0.02224, χ²_cc=5.16, **CI95 [+0.012,+0.108] excludes zero**, `RECOVERY_LIFT_SIGNIFICANT=TRUE`, 300-row paired Table+Artifact) at only **~−2.6% TPS — 6× cheaper than int8**. BUT absolute 0.4167 greedy / 0.3967 sampled lands **one item / 0.023 under the 0.420 gate**, inside this harness's ~0.05 calibration offset → the absolute call is fragile; the **paired lift is the robust, basis-independent finding**. So g32-on-locus is the cheap within-mandate recovery knob but does **NOT cleanly moot int8**. W&B [`5glqi1m1`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/5glqi1m1) (locus 0.4167) / [`vgnk3k5z`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/vgnk3k5z) (anchor 0.350) / [`gun5dbu6`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/gun5dbu6) (paired McNemar). With the GEMV leg (wirbel #736), the strict-#319 divergence is now **fully attributed and byte-exact-fixable** → the G1-immune (zero-DQ) byte-exact spec ship path is viable. | field | bi0 (num_splits>1) | bi1 (num_splits=1) |
|---|---|---|
| layer0 attn_out frac_bitdiff | 1.0 | **0.0** |
| e2e argmax-flip rate | 0.0104 | **0** |
| first-divergent-locus layer | 0 | **none (all 42 byte-exact)** |
| prelmhead max_abs delta | 1.875 | **0** |
| AR-vs-AR control | 1.000000 | 1.000000 |

Since the #515 gate is "≥90% of vanilla base," the AIME bar is denominator-dependent: at base 0.4667 → bar **0.420** (full-g32 0.438 PASSES, int8 moot); at base 0.50@gb6144 → bar **0.45** (full-g32 0.438 FAILS, int8-locus 0.450 sole lane). **Reseat — denken #723 (`denken/aime-gate-bar-shiprisk`, LOCAL A10G eval on `.venvs/vllm022`, analysis-only):** pin the gate denominator and quantify clearance with the **right** instrument. But at the clean budget (12288, `fl≈0` both sides) the shipped int4 body still lands **0.400 < 0.420** and −0.083 vs bf16 0.4833, and the matched 5-seed **sampled** CIs are **non-overlapping** (int4-AR hi 0.4022 < bf16 lo 0.4045). The AIME gap is **body-level, not truncation/sampling**. Reconciles denken #637's 0.400 (the 0.350↔0.400 delta was the 6144 cap all along). This is the **binding quality blocker** for Option-B.
- **Arm B — bar moved the *wrong way* for the card's hope:** the single 0.5404 bf16 GPQA-D seed was *low*; the seed-robust **10-seed pooled mean = 0.5561 (1101/1980), Wilson [0.5341, 0.5778]** (run `pr650-harden-gpqa-bf16-10seed`), so the 0.9× bar **RISES 0.4864 → 0.5005** and int4-AR's 0.499 flips #638's marginal-pass → **−0.0015 fail**. Accepted honest framing: at −0.0015 (inside seed noise) this is a **tie, not a decisive fail** — the *sign* is robust (all 10 bf16 seeds ≥ the old anchor) but the point margin is a coin-flip. Either way it removes int4-AR's one marginal GPQA pass. (`int4ar_0p499_clears_new_bar=False`, `optionb_0p465_clears_new_bar=False`.)
- **Net for the programme:** both hardenings push the #481 quality reading the same way — the int4 body genuinely misses the AIME bar (Arm A binding, CI-clean, body-level; Arm B downgrades a marginal pass to a tie). | bar (M=1 AR, drafter OFF, greedy, gb6144, BI=1) | bf16 base | QAT-int4 (live body) | **ALT GPTQ-int4** | scheme-Δ (alt−QAT) | gate |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| **AIME** (n=60) — BINDING | 0.4667 | 0.4000 (85.7%) | **0.4000 (85.7%)** | **0.0000** McNemar p=1.000 | **FAIL** |
| GPQA-D (n=198) — clears | 0.4899 | 0.4798 (97.9%) | **0.5051 (103.1%)** | +0.0253 McNemar p=0.560 n.s. | PASS |

- **The 4-bit AIME ceiling is scheme-independent.** Swapping QAT→GPTQ moved AIME by **exactly nothing** (0.4000=0.4000); the two schemes solve *different* problems (share 18/24, each unique 6 → b=c=6 perfectly symmetric churn) at an *identical rate*. denken **directly tested the calibration lever** (reasoning-rich CoT) — also moved nothing → the deficit reads as a **representational-capacity limit of 4-bit weights on long-chain competition math**, not a rounding/calib artifact a better PTQ recipe fixes.
- **int4 is NOT a blanket reasoning killer:** on GPQA-D the alt scheme sits *at/above* bf16 (103.1%) — corroborates GPQA model-limited-not-precision-limited (fern #646, wirbel #652).
- **Synthesis across the attack:** fern #646 (vertical/more-bits: int8 ¼-recovers, bf16 full) + denken #647 (horizontal/scheme: identical) ⇒ the int4-g128 AIME deficit is **neither recipe-recoverable nor cheaply uniform-bit-recoverable**. The two live levers left: per-layer precision **allocation** (fern #659) + group size **g32** (lawine #653, correctly deferred to). Hardens Reading-B on the binding fail; **no shippable-int4 quality lever hides behind the recipe at g128.**

## 2026-06-18 ~10:30Z — Cycle-58BJ: bank fern #646 **`INT8_PARTIAL`** (the int4→int8→bf16 reasoning ladder — GPQA is model-limited not precision-limited; AIME is the lone binding bar, bit-monotone but only ¼-recoverable by uniform int8) + reseat **fern → #659** (AIME precision-*allocation* lower bound). **★ ANALYSIS-ONLY TERMINAL BANKED (closed, NOT merged — `analysis_only=true`/`official_tps=0`; int8 is *dominated* — slower than int4 AND still fails AIME — so no ship candidate, nothing for the served path; locked 126.378 rung untouched).**

**#646 (fern) — BANKED (closed, NOT merged), `INT8_PARTIAL`, W&B-verified (all 3 runs reconcile exactly via a wandb-query sub-agent, all `analysis_only=true`/`official_tps=0`/`finished`; the relog from local summary JSONs is faithful — `ladder/`-prefixed fields == top-level on every run).** The bit-width-only ladder (int8 = the bf16 base `google/gemma-4-E4B-it@fee6332c` quantized W8A16 g128 body+head, 343 modules, identical 2765-tensor skeleton to int4; only `num_bits` differs 4↔8) cleanly separates the two failing bars:

| bar | int4 | int8 | bf16 | bit-monotone? | binding? |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| GPQA-D (n=198, greedy) | 0.5000 (`ui02lj1g`) | **0.5657** (`nijokiln`) | 0.4899 (`g3cig1xo`) | **NO** (int8>int4>bf16) | no — all clear, model-limited |
| AIME (n=60, greedy) | 0.4000 (`dh0tbwpp`) | **0.4167** (`jz3ojbio`) | 0.4667 (`zoszxnb0`) | **YES** (int4<int8<bf16) | **YES — int8 still FAILS the 0.420 bar** |

- **GPQA-D is model-limited, NOT precision-limited.** int4/int8/bf16 all clear both 90% bars and the ordering is *not* bit-monotone (int8 0.5657 > int4 0.5000 > bf16 0.4899). Direct n=198 evidence that greedy GPQA-Diamond is noise/model-limited — **corroborates wirbel #652 `RECIPE_EFFECT_IS_SMALL_AR`** ([`1rp6aanq`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/1rp6aanq)): a GPQA "deficit" appears only in the sampled-with-spec regime, never the AR body.
- **AIME is the genuine binding bar — bit-monotone but only weakly recoverable.** int8 closes just **¼ of the int4→bf16 gap** (one item, 24→25/60, for 2× the bits) → the recovery knee sits near bf16, not int8. **Corroborates ubel #650 Arm A `AIME_DEFICIT_GENUINE`** — the AIME deficit is real at clean budget AND isn't cheaply bought back with uniform bits. Honest caveat accepted: AIME n=60 pct-CI [64%,116%] is huge, so the load-bearing read is the ¼-gap *slope*, not the single-item miss. (int8 AIME per-year: 2024 15/30, 2025-I 5/15, 2025-II 5/15 — new-set degradation; 8/60 = 13.3% truncated runaways, all-wrong, apples-to-apples under the shared gb6144 budget.)
- **fern's honest cross-finding (right, already banked team-wide):** her in-harness int4-GPQA greedy 0.5000 = 102.1% does NOT reproduce the "~86%" int4-GPQA figure — that figure is the **sampled/Option-B-spec** number (fern #629 [`2jhhk0u3`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/2jhhk0u3)) vs a different denominator. Under a self-consistent greedy ladder int4-GPQA clears — the GPQA half of "int4 loses graduate reasoning" does NOT hold under greedy; **AIME is where the real int4 deficit lives.**

**Reseat: fern → #659** `aime-precision-allocation-lb` — fern's own #1 follow-up, sharpened. The uniform-precision question is closed (uniform int8 isn't enough); the open lever is **per-layer precision *allocation*** — explicitly flagged by land #619 (`FUNDAMENTAL_PRECISION_LOSS`, [`lrlbhtuf`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/lrlbhtuf): "remaining deficit requires model-side improvements… per-layer precision mixing") and pointed at by the collapse-locus line (stark #536 `collapse_locus=BODY`, ubel #538 layer-removal-dominant, ubel #546 depth-knee=2, kanna #539 bake-damage). Card measures the **lower bound**: the *minimum* layer-upgrade (int4→int8/bf16 on the top-N AIME-collapse layers) that recovers AIME ≥0.420, and its TPS price. Verdict `AIME_RECOVERABLE_CHEAP` (small N at tolerable cost → **SURFACE** to human as a quality-recovering Option-B-body candidate; the int4-QAT *mandate*-compatibility ruling is the human's, NOT an autonomous fire) / `AIME_RECOVERABLE_EXPENSIVE` (needs near-bf16 → AIME deficit is precision-intrinsic, the **quality-recovery axis CLOSES**) / `AIME_IRRECOVERABLE` (loss isn't in the identified layers). LOCAL only (`analysis_only`/`official_tps=0`, NO HF Job — does NOT auto-fire per the #481 guardrail).

**★ The correction (process lesson banked to memory).** My Cycle-58BH 09:31Z "ubel #650 DARK POD" + 09:40Z "lawine #653 DARK" escalations were **wrong**. Both were built on a W&B sweep showing "no new run today" — but ubel proved its pod ALIVE at 09:41Z with hard proof-of-life (running PIDs `2044592`/`2004575`/`2012236`, **GPU0 100% util**, fresh on-disk artifacts 06:14–09:13Z). The pod had been grinding a ~4.5 h GPQA panel the whole time and had simply chosen to **log the whole W&B group in one pass at the end** (a watcher-unfriendly but legitimate choice it has since fixed by logging the panel-so-far, group `int4ar-denominator-harden-ubel`). **W&B-absence is a visibility signal, not a liveness signal.** Actions: swapped #650 `status:review`→`status:wip` (the review label was premature — Arm B is 2/10, no terminal marker), posted an apology+acceptance on #650, **softened lawine #653 to a heartbeat request** (stood down the kubectl ask pending proof-of-life — it may be the same false positive), and **saved a feedback memory** (`dark-pod-escalation-discipline`: do a graduated heartbeat/proof-of-life check before escalating; model Morgan's 08:08Z "liveness check — not an escalation yet").

**ubel #650 Arm A — ACCEPTED, `AIME_DEFICIT_GENUINE` (the AIME-truncation critical-path blocker, RESOLVED).** Fresh within-session bf16 vs int4-AR, greedy, `max_tokens ∈ {6144, 8192, 12288}`, `mml=16384` so the cap is the only moving part (group `int4ar-denominator-harden-ubel`, runs `2bbsv9xr`/`94enwbpp`/`t4limybq` + budget/flcurve runs, `analysis_only`):

| budget | bf16 acc / fl / cens | int4-AR acc / fl / cens |
|---|---|---|
| 6144 | 0.4833 / 0.150 / 0.5686 | 0.3167 / 0.133 / 0.3654 |
| 8192 | 0.4833 / 0.017 / 0.4915 | 0.3667 / 0.017 / 0.3729 |
| 12288 | 0.4833 / 0.000 / 0.4833 | **0.4000** / 0.017 / 0.4068 |

bf16 is **budget-insensitive** (flat 0.4833 — its truncated chains were wrong anyway). int4-AR **climbs +0.083** (0.317→0.400) as `fl→0`, so a real chunk of the raw-6144 gap *was* the 6144 cutoff. **But at the clean budget (12288, `fl≈0` both) int4-AR = 0.400 still sits −0.020 UNDER the 0.420 bar** and 0.083 below bf16; even charging the lone remaining truncated int4-AR problem as correct → 25/60 = 0.4167 < 0.420; and 5-seed **sampled** @12288 = 0.3467, Wilson [0.2951, 0.4022], **CI upper < 0.420**. → **Truncation amplified the AIME gap but did not create it; the literal-gate-breach-via-AIME survives the correction.** ★ Bonus: ubel's @12288 = 0.400 **reconciles the long-standing ubel-0.350-vs-denken-#637-0.400 discrepancy** — denken's was the higher-budget number; the gap was the 6144 truncation cap all along. (Interim only — #650 stays WIP pending the Arm B terminal.)

**ubel #650 Arm B (2/10, directional — MATERIAL) — the 10-seed bf16 GPQA bar is RISING.** Byte-identical protocol to the #638 int4-AR 10-seed leg. Seeds done: s0=0.5758, s1=0.5707 → **pooled 0.5732** (n=396, Wilson [0.524, 0.621]) — both **above** the 0.5404 single-seed anchor, so the 0.9× bar **rises 0.4864 → ~0.5159**, and on that partial bar int4-AR's 0.499 GPQA **flips to a marginal FAIL (−0.0169)**. Not over-read at 2 seeds, but this is the read that could move GPQA-sampled from marginal-pass to marginal-fail — **if it holds at 10 seeds, the int4-AR body fails the #515 quality gate on BOTH AIME and GPQA in the sampled regime** (greedy GPQA 0.5051 still clears; this is the sampled/lewtun-#31 axis). Carry to the full 10-seed terminal (~13:15Z) + terminal `SENPAI-RESULT`.

**land #657 CLOSED (duplicate) → land #658 reseated.** ubel Arm A answers the AIME-truncation census decisively, so the land #657 routing I created last cycle (when I wrongly thought ubel was dark) is redundant — closed it ([issuecomment-4740736536](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/pull/657)). | corner | body | head | GPQA-D sampled | run |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| A | g32 | bf16-tied | 0.5056 (93.6%) | lawine #639 `r98w09by` |
| B | g32 | int4-untied | 0.5152 (95.3%) | lawine #639 `k7bxh4tw` |
| **C** | **g128** | **bf16-tied** | **0.4753** (88.0%) | **wirbel #652 `1rp6aanq`** |
| D | g128 | int4-untied | 0.4990 (92.3%) | ubel #638 `u13z29hs` |

- **The recipe axis barely moves AR-GPQA.** All three pure-AR corners cluster in **[0.475, 0.506]** — a 0.030 spread inside Wilson noise (±0.022 @ n=1980). Corner C is statistically indistinguishable from A (p=0.056) and D (p=0.135).
- **`QUALITY_IS_HEAD_DRIVEN` is REFUTED.** Head effect @ g128 (C−D) = **−0.0237 (p=0.135, n.s.)** — the bf16 head does NOT recover quality; if anything C is the lowest corner. Body effect @ bf16-head (A−C) = +0.0303 (p=0.056) leans body but doesn't clear significance.
- **★ HEADLINE: the GPQA "deficit" 0.4652 is a SPEC effect, NOT the recipe.** The 0.4652 that motivated the whole quality-recovery thread is the **Option-B-WITH-SPEC sampled** number (fern #629 `2jhhk0u3`) — the wrong denominator. In apples-to-apples pure-AR terms every corner sits ~0.50 and clears (or sits on) the 0.4864 bar. The recipe/group-size/head levers are NOT where GPQA quality is won or lost — the only place a GPQA gap appears is the **sampled-with-spec** regime.

Dovetails with lawine #639 (`RECIPE_RECOVERS_GPQA` — group size is the GPQA lever but the live deficit is on AIME) and wirbel #649 (`G32_QUALITY_SAFE_BUT_SLOW` — the head is a pure speed knob). **Net: the within-recipe AR-GPQA quality knobs are EXHAUSTED**, GPQA is fine under the greedy gate that matters (#319 byte-exact → served stream == AR, ~0.499/0.505), and the one open GPQA question is whether the sampled-with-spec 0.4652 is a real spec cost or low-seed noise. ## 2026-06-18 ~08:30Z — Cycle-58BE: bank land #654 **`TIE_TOLERANT_RESIDUAL`** (served-identity EXACT upgrade — closed as IRREDUCIBLE at the int4 quantization-noise floor; the durable asset is the batch-M-stable `ar_ref_m1_canonical` oracle). **★ ANALYSIS-ONLY TERMINAL BANKED (offline `research/` artifact path — nothing to merge into the served path; `analysis_only=true`/`official_tps=0`, locked 126.378 rung untouched). The #319 served-identity leg cannot be upgraded to literal "exact," but the reason it can't IS the airtight closure: the residual is a property of the int4 quantization grid, not of any one reference, so no oracle choice can drive it to 0 — and it is bounded at exactly one int4 quantum (≤0.125 nat canonical / ≤0.25 nat verify) with 0 confident misses (>0.5 nat) across all 4 507 fired head positions and all of K=3/5/7.**

**#654 (land) — BANKED (closed, NOT merged — offline analysis on a `research/` artifact path), `TIE_TOLERANT_RESIDUAL`, W&B-verified ([`ah3fe0h1`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ah3fe0h1) finished, group `served-identity-exact-land`, `analysis_only=true`/`official_tps=0`, every field reconciles).** The attempted upgrade of #651 from *tie-tolerance* to *EXACT*. Two literal upgrade routes, both fail — but the failure is the proof of irreducibility:

| route | precondition | result |
|---|---|---|
| `SERVED_IDENTITY_EXACT` | AR-wins-all-60 ties **or** head-break→0 | ✗ (55/60; K5 head break 3.16%) |
| `EXACT_BY_TIEBREAK_ONLY` | AR-wins-all-60 ties | ✗ (5 lost, 3 unique loci) |
| **`TIE_TOLERANT_RESIDUAL`** ✓ | bounded irreducible residual | **✓** |

**Part 1 — tie-break direction audit (offline, free):** the recompute acceptor resolves exact ties by lowest token index (`torch.argmax` first index). At the 60 wide head ties (recompute_margin 0.125–0.25 nat), AR wins iff `a_pos < r_pos` → **55/60 AR wins** (K3 22/24, K5 19/20, K7 14/16), **0 degenerate**. The 5 losses are 3 unique loci (one repeats at two K): `gpqa_diamond-019eb0f05f` pos31, `mmlu_pro-00ebfcaa67` pos485, `mmlu_pro-00996c6808` pos98 — all vmarg/rcmarg 0.125–0.25. AR does NOT win all 60 → the AR-wins-all precondition fails.

**Part 2 — batch-M-stable canonical M=1 decode oracle** (`research/walltps_ab/optionb_bi1_stock_int4/ksweep/ar_ref_m1_canonical/`, 128 records, output_len 512, 65 536 tokens, 871 s; does NOT overwrite `ar_ref_bi1`; `logprobs=20` captured DURING generation for faithful decode-path margins). Re-ran the #651 on-AR-head census against it:

| K | on-AR head break_rate | confident misses (>0.5 nat) | served-vs-canonical residuals (all ≤0.125 nat) |
|---|---|---|---|
| 3 | 55/1613 = 3.41% | **0** | 4 |
| **5** | 50/1583 = **3.16%** | **0** | **2** |
| 7 | 29/1311 = 2.21% | **0** | 5 |

**The head break does NOT drop to 0** — essentially unchanged from #651's 2.81% (K5). The wide breaks were NOT pure `ar_ref_bi1` artifacts: the canonical oracle hits the same int4 ties. **11 genuine served-vs-canonical residuals total, every one ≤0.125-nat canonical / ≤0.25-nat verify on BOTH sides, 0 confident misses.** Irreducibility proof: comparing the new oracle to `ar_ref_bi1`, **18/128 prompts diverge and 18/18 diverge at a canonical near-tie (≤0.25 nat)**; two independent batch-invariant M=1 decode runs land on opposite sides of the same ~0.125-nat tie. → the residual is a property of the **quantization grid** (prefill M=len, decode-run-A, decode-run-B, verify M=K+1 each pick a side; none is "more canonical"), so a better oracle cannot drive the head break to 0. **What IS invariant and airtight: every divergence = one int4 quantum, 0 confident misses across 4 507 fired head positions, invariant to reference choice → quantization-noise floor.**

**Durable asset:** the `ar_ref_m1_canonical` oracle is now the standard batch-M-stable reference for all future served-identity census (#645/#648/#651), documented canonical-up-to-int4-ties (18/128 prompts tie-ambiguous → treat the ≤0.25-nat near-tie band as a don't-care set in any identity gate). Repro scripts in `research/walltps_ab/optionb_bi1_stock_int4/rescue_census/` (`tiebreak_audit.py`, `gen_canonical_oracle.py --output-len 512`, `check_oracle_consistency.py`, `recensus_canonical.py`, `residual_margins.py`, `log_654_wandb.py`). Code diff is offline analysis + a new reusable artifact dir under `research/`; no served-file or build change. **Student follow-ups noted for future:** (1) lowest-index tie-break at *exact* verify ties (vmarg 0.0) → would convert the construction-safe 55/60 majority into a deterministic guarantee, shrinking the residual to ≤5 index-losses; (2) price the 11 residuals in PPL/answer terms → numeric quality bound for #319; (3) adopt `ar_ref_m1_canonical` as standard reference. Bank comment + close on #654.

| gate | answer_div_rate | net graded Δ (95% CI) | McNemar p | large-margin flips |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GPQA | 0.298 (59/198) | **−0.0101** [−0.0707, +0.0505] ∋0 | 0.871 | 0 |
| GSM8K | 0.054 (27/500) | **−0.0020** [−0.0160, +0.0120] ∋0 | 1.0 | 0 |

- **★ Mechanism REFUTED but conclusion HOLDS.** The card hypothesized flips stay in the CoT mid-band and never touch the boxed answer — **refuted:** greedy *cascades* a single mid-CoT tie-flip down the AR stream, so ~30% of GPQA / ~5% of GSM8K final answers differ. **But the cost is net-zero via a symmetric coin-flip:** every root divergence is a `<0.5-nat` int4 grid-tie (`frac_flips_under_0p5nat=1.0`), `pooled/n_large_margin_answer_flips_total=0`, the answer shuffle adds/removes correct answers in balance, both McNemar symmetric (b≈c), τ=0.3 nat erases 99.5–100%. (~70% of token-flips self-heal — CoT reconverges to the same boxed letter.) Completes the greedy+sampled pair (sampled #620 also net-null: GPQA −0.0081 / GSM8K +0.0032 ∋0).
- **★ Clean answer for the tolerance call:** the strict-#319 break is **real at the byte level (0.40%/step, 5–30% of answers shuffle) but materially free in graded accuracy** — symmetric, zero large-margin, all grid-ties, τ=0.3-rescuable. **Firing Option-B costs only the strict byte-exact #319 greedy contract, not graded quality.**

**Reseat — wirbel → #633** `optionb-draft-bi-detax` (my novel lever, the highest-value follow-on): in greedy spec-dec the OUTPUT tokens are fixed *entirely* by the TARGET verify forward (accepted tokens are target-argmax; the bonus is target-argmax) → the draft head's determinism is **irrelevant to output**, only to acceptance length. So #319 byte-exactness needs BI=1 **only on the target**, and the **draft head can run BI=0 (fast 3D split-KV)**. Since land #623 localized ≥85% of the −40% tax to the M=1 draft forwards, BI=0-draft + BI=1-target should **recover most of the 152.29→253.99 gap while staying byte-exact** — and it **sidesteps** the "no free faster byte-exact GEMM on sm_86" wall (#630/#423/#550) because we stop paying BI on the forward whose determinism doesn't matter. Scoped gated: (1) theory-confirm draft-output-invariance (cheap, no patch), (2) feasibility — is `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT` draft-selectable or process-global (context-manager toggle LOC estimate, harness-only, NO submission change), (3) measure-if-feasible (Arm A BI=1-both 152.29 anchor vs Arm B BI=0-draft+BI=1-target; net wall_tps + **#319 byte-identity n≥64 0-flips gate** + acceptance + PPL). Verdict `DRAFT_BI_DETAX_VIABLE` / `DRAFT_BI_GLOBAL_ONLY` / `DRAFT_AFFECTS_OUTPUT`. Orthogonal to + compounds with land #632 (K = forward COUNT; draft-BI = per-forward tax). analysis_only, official_tps=0, NO HF Job.

## 2026-06-17 ~20:35Z — Cycle-58AL: bank stark #617 (BI-Marlin coverage audit) — **CORRECTS the spec-#319 break mechanism: int4-Marlin is bit-exact at the deployed verify width (M≤16, maxdiff 0.0, all four served shapes), so wirbel #607's 47% break is a NON-Marlin op (prime suspect: attention varlen paged-KV combine — converges with competitor fabulous-frenzy's independent "consumed attention positions still diverge" report). Reseat stark → #621 (localize the real trigger; if it is a RECOVERABLE serving/config issue → potential option-B strict-#319 rescue with a FLAG, not a kernel build — a ~3.4× unlock over the locked 126.378). NO FIRE; #481 GPQA-body HOLD unchanged.**

**Theme — the standing mechanism for the spec break was wrong, and the correction reopens a high-upside conditional lever.** Through Cycle-58AK the fleet attributed wirbel #607's 31048/65536 (47%) greedy break to int4-Marlin M-dependence (M=8 verify ≠ M=1 AR). stark #617 refutes that **at the deployed verify width**: the int4-Marlin GEMM is bit-exact (maxdiff 0.0) at M=7/8/16 on all four served fused shapes; first divergence appears only at M=32 (~1 bf16-ULP). The submission runs `MAX_NUM_SEQS=1`, K=6/7 → verify width M=7/8, so **Marlin contributes ZERO divergence and is not even the first domino.** A 47% break is categorically larger than the sub-1% near-tie argmax flips a ULP-magnitude GEMM diff could produce → the trigger is a **non-Marlin op** in the spec-verify path, prime suspect the attention varlen paged-KV combine (which converges with competitor fabulous-frenzy's independently-reported "consumed attention positions still diverge"). **UNCHANGED** by this correction: the top-line "spec breaks strict #319 on this int4 substrate" conclusion (wirbel #607) holds; the M>1 verify *speed* tax (~2.7× an M=1 step, denken #609) is a throughput property and stands; the GPQA-body finding is untouched (different axis). **The conditional upside (drives stark #621):** *if* the non-Marlin trigger is a *recoverable* serving/config issue — e.g. the spec-verify attention silently NOT taking the `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT` `num_splits=1` batch-invariant route — rather than a fundamental kernel limit, then option B (~427 TPS MTP candidate) could be made strict-#319 byte-exact with a **flag, not a kernel build**, a ~3.4× unlock over the locked-in 126.378. Conditional, not a claim; #621 returns the verdict, which I will SURFACE to the human the moment it lands (per #481 — non-byte-identical never auto-fires). **Public board: posted a correction to fabulous-frenzy retracting the Marlin-argmax-flip mechanism I'd given them (it converges with their own attention finding) — honest fix > letting a wrong root-cause propagate cross-team.**

### stark #617 — BANKED+MERGED — `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1` routes around the int4-Marlin GEMM, BUT Marlin is already bit-exact at the verify width → the spec-#319 break is a NON-Marlin op
- stark; run [`fa1f9vm1`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/fa1f9vm1), group `bi-marlin-coverage-audit`, `finished`. **W&B-verified — `marlin_m_sweep` table maxdiff 0.0 at M=7/8/16 on all four served shapes, `any_shape_diverges_at_verify_width=false`, first divergence M=32 (gate_up ~9.8e-4) / M=128 (down ~2.4e-4), `proof_BI_active_marlin_untouched=true`; `analysis_only=true`/`official_tps=0`, no submission keys, ~1.5 GiB peak (synthetic int4 weights — NO checkpoint, NO kernel build, ENOSPC-safe).** Bank comment issuecomment-4734994586.

| served shape | K | N | maxdiff M=7 | M=8 | M=16 | first divergence |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| qkv | 2560 | 3072 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | none (≤128) |
| o_proj | 2048 | 2560 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | none (≤128) |
| gate_up | 2560 | 20480 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | M=32 (~9.8e-4, 1 bf16-ULP) |
| down | 10240 | 2560 | 0.0 | 0.0 | 0.0 | M=128 (~2.4e-4, 1 bf16-ULP) |

- **VERDICT — `MARLIN_OUTSIDE_BI__REQUIRES_CUSTOM_KERNEL`, with the rider that flips the action.** Two questions cleanly separated, both verified against the raw run: **(1) Coverage (structural) — confirmed:** `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1` installs a single `aten`-IMPL `torch.library.Library` overriding only dense ATen ops (`mm`/`addmm`/`matmul`/`linear` Triton-persistent on SM80-family, `bmm`, softmax/mean) + attention `num_splits=1` (`flash_attn.py:1194/1219`); it registers **NO `torch.ops._C.*` override**, so the int4-Marlin custom op (`_C.marlin_gemm`, dispatched via `CompressedTensorsWNA16 → MarlinLinearKernel → apply_gptq_marlin_linear`) is routed entirely around it. Runtime proof: enabling BI moved `aten::mm` bits (maxdiff 1.95e-3) while `_C.marlin_gemm` was bit-identical (0.0) before vs after. **(2) Attribution (causal) — REFUTED, load-bearing:** Marlin is *already* bit-exact at the deployed verify width (table above), so it contributes zero divergence to the spec-verify-vs-AR comparison. **Action: do NOT open a custom batch-invariant Marlin kernel build** — zero identity gain at the verify width, #319-class risk for nothing. There is also no in-wheel *fast* byte-exact BI int4 path on sm_86 (the only BI-covered GEMM is dense `aten::mm` = the ~48% dequant-to-bf16 tax the int4 stack exists to avoid). The 47% break originates in a non-Marlin op → **reseated stark → #621** to localize it with the same row-0 bit-exact probe (M=8 vs M=1) on (a) attention varlen paged-KV combine, (b) MTP drafter forward, (c) sampler/argmax tie-break.

### Mechanism correction propagated to Cycle-58AK
The Cycle-58AK entries banked the spec-#319 break with the then-best mechanistic attribution of "int4-Marlin M-dependence." stark #617 refutes that **at the verify width** (Marlin maxdiff 0.0 at M≤16). Inline `[CORRECTED #617]` markers added to the three affected claims below (Cycle-58AK theme (1), the wirbel #607 entry, the denken #609 entry). The *conclusions* those entries reached are unchanged — spec breaks strict #319 (wirbel #607), draft-free ngram is net-negative (denken #609), and the M>1 verify speed tax stands — **only the named trigger moves from int4-Marlin to a non-Marlin (attention-suspect) op.**

- **stark → #621** — localize the non-Marlin spec-#319 trigger on vLLM 0.22.0 with #617's row-0 bit-exact probe (output row 0, M=8 vs M=1), **NO build, NO HF Job, single A10G**: (1) **attention varlen paged-KV combine (prime suspect)** — trace the `int4_mtp_batchinv` submission's actual attention backend at the M=8 spec-verify step and confirm whether the `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT` `num_splits=1` guard (`flash_attn.py:1194/1219`) is *actually taken* at verify width, or a spec-verify TRITON_ATTN / 3D split-KV redirect bypasses it; (2) if attention is clean, **MTP drafter forward + sampler/argmax tie-break**.
