## 2026-06-17 ~19:52Z (+denken #609 banked ~20:04Z) — Cycle-58AK: 5-terminal analysis bank — **the speed frontier is CONVERGED and the GPQA softness is an int4-BODY property, shared by option A AND option B (not spec-specific); spec is distribution-preserving under SAMPLED decoding (so option B's only quality cost is the GREEDY #319 contract, not downstream eval quality).** Banked stark #613 (**option C CLOSED** — sparse-Marlin fully removed from vLLM dev307: scheme gone, op gone, parser raises → 126.378 is the locked quality-safe AR ceiling for the weight-compression axis) + wirbel #607 (**spec STRUCTURALLY BREAKS #319 on the 0.22.0 pin** — clean 0/65536 cross-start floor, cand diverges 31048/65536 tokens, *slightly worse* than fern #597's separate-boot 0.3125 → refutes the venv-noise hypothesis) + kanna #610 (**AR int4_g128_lmhead GPQA-Diamond DECISIVE-FAIL** — 10-seed mean 0.4414 CI95 [0.4176,0.4652] entirely below 0.4712; de-bias 6144 did NOT lift; + served greedy NOT run-to-run reproducible at conc=16, 64/198 flips) + land #598 (**int4-BODY is a significant GPQA regression vs vanilla base** — gpqa_main n=448 McNemar exact p=0.0009, head-invariant; + the gate is **un-CI-robustifiable** on any GPQA split) + denken #609 (**draft-free ngram/prompt-lookup spec-decode is NET-NEGATIVE on the int4 substrate** — −16% TPS in *every* swept config, breaks #319 via the same M>1 int4-Marlin mechanism; but the load-bearing positive: GSM8K quality HOLDS, 0.890 spec vs 0.884 AR-same-substrate Δ+0.006 within SE≈0.014 → **spec-dec with rejection sampling is distribution-preserving under sampled decoding**). All five W&B-verified (`eqvdyntw`/`yuvztndu`/`gho8wxxs`/`n4ro7bzk`/`7gzefuwk`: finished, `official_tps=0`/`analysis_only`, no submission keys, headline numbers exact). Reseated all five idle students into a deliberately **validity-heavy + option-B-rescue** portfolio (frontier converged → the binding question is now the denominator and whether option B can be rescued): wirbel→#616 (raw teacher-forced structural flip-rate), stark→#617 (BI-Marlin coverage audit), kanna→#618 (GPQA conc=1-vs-16 determinism), land→#619 (int4-body GPQA failure-mode error analysis), denken→#620 (matched-arm spec-distribution-preservation on the MTP candidate + GPQA-D — the decisive piece for the human's option-B fire decision). Roster 8/8, 0 idle. **NO FIRE** (operative submission remains int4_g128_lmhead @ 126.378). **#481 HOLD: do NOT re-surface "option A is GPQA-soft" to the human until fern #612 (gen-budget) + lawine #615 (dev307-accuracy-validity) converge — all three GPQA reads were on dev307; surfacing-then-retracting would whipsaw the already-made decision to lock 126.378.**

**Theme — three independent reads converged on one mechanism, and the speed axis is exhausted on this stack.** The cycle banked four analysis cards that together close the speed frontier and re-frame the quality picture. **(1) Speed is converged:** option A (shipped int4_g128_lmhead @ 126.378) is the strict-#319 HBM ceiling; option B (spec int4+MTP ~427.7) **structurally breaks #319** (wirbel #607, on the correct 0.22.0 pin — the int4-Marlin GEMM is M-dependent, M=8 verify ≠ M=1 AR, and the divergence is *worse* on the clean deterministic gate, killing the "it's just the venv" reopening) **[CORRECTED #617: the spec-#319 trigger is NOT int4-Marlin — the GEMM is bit-exact at the M=7/8 verify width (maxdiff 0.0, all 4 shapes); the break is a non-Marlin op (attention-suspect, stark #621). The "spec structurally breaks #319" conclusion is unchanged.]**; option C (sub-int4 weight compression) is **DEAD** (stark #613 — the sparse-Marlin kernel is fully excised from dev307 on all three layers: no `W4A16Sparse24` scheme, no `gptq_marlin_24_gemm` op, and the vendored parser actively raises on any sparsity config). The only remaining strict-#319-compatible faster config would require a batch-invariant int4-Marlin kernel — which is exactly what stark #617 is now auditing for feasibility. **[CORRECTED #617: a BI-Marlin kernel would fix NOTHING — Marlin is already bit-exact at the M=7/8 verify width (maxdiff 0.0). The real spec-#319 trigger is a non-Marlin op (stark #621 localizing); IF it is a recoverable serving/config issue, option B could become strict-#319 byte-exact with a flag, not a kernel build — see Cycle-58AL.]** **(2) The GPQA softness is the int4 BODY, not spec:** kanna #610's AR GPQA-D 0.4414, stark #605's spec int4+MTP 0.4141, and land #598's isolated int4-body gpqa_main regression (McNemar p=0.0009, head-invariant per stark #536) are three reads of the *same* deficit. So the GPQA gate is a property of the int4 body shared by the shipped config AND the spec candidate — it is not a reason to prefer one option over the other on quality. **(3) The gate is un-CI-robustifiable** (land #598: no GPQA split — Diamond 198 / Main 448 / Extended 546 — is large enough for a CI-lb-clears-90% gate; n needed ~5240), so the 90%-of-base GPQA gate can only ever be a policy point/seed-mean gate. **(4) Spec is distribution-preserving under SAMPLED decoding** (denken #609: matched-arm ngram GSM8K 0.890 vs AR-same-substrate 0.884, Δ+0.006 within SE≈0.014 — rejection sampling preserves the *target* distribution at T>0). This is the reframe that re-opens option B on quality: the #319 break is a *greedy byte-exact* phenomenon, but the SHIPPED downstream evals (#31) run *sampled* — so if spec's downstream eval distribution = plain-AR int4's (denken #620 tests exactly this on the MTP candidate + GPQA-D), then firing option B costs ONLY the strict-#319 greedy contract, not downstream quality, and the GPQA softness is purely the int4 body, neither helped nor hurt by spec. **The one load-bearing caveat (the reason for the #481 HOLD): all three GPQA reads were measured on dev307**, the exact stack lawine #615 is auditing — lawine #606 already proved dev307 is +30% PPL-inflated and 100% decode-divergent vs the 0.22.0 submission, so if dev307 *also* distorts accuracy, these GPQA numbers re-base. A paired within-stack McNemar (land #598) is more robust to a stack-wide offset than an absolute-threshold gate, so the *significance* likely survives even if the *magnitude* shifts — but the consolidated #481 update waits for #615 + fern #612.

Mechanism = the SAME int4-Marlin M-dependence as #607: realizable acceptance on the real sharegpt mix is only 25.2% (M=1, E[L]=2.25 tok/step), but each verify pass runs the int4-Marlin body at M=K+1≈6, costing ~2.7× an M=1 AR step — 2.25 tokens cannot pay for a 2.7× step → net loss. **[CLARIFIED #617: the SPEED tax here (M>1 verify ≈ 2.7× an M=1 step) is REAL and UNCHANGED — a throughput property. #617 corrects only the IDENTITY attribution: int4-Marlin is bit-exact in *value* at M=7/8 (maxdiff 0.0), so the #319 break — both #607's and the 99/128 divergence below — is a non-Marlin op, not the GEMM. Speed-M-dependence ≠ value-M-dependence; the net-negative speed verdict stands.]** The warm `probe_tps` 4× "win" (446–508 TPS) is a single self-referential near-100%-copy prompt, not the workload. **So on int4, M>1 spec is *doubly* penalized — it breaks #319 AND it's too slow.** (2) **Identity: BREAKS strict #319** (99/128 prompts divergent), exactly as #607 predicts, with a clean 0/128 AR-vs-AR' cross-start floor proving the divergence is the spec path, not env noise. (3) **★ Quality: distribution-preserving — GSM8K spec 0.890 vs AR-same-substrate 0.884, Δ+0.006 within SE≈0.014.** This is the matched-arm empirical confirmation that spec-dec with rejection sampling preserves the target distribution under sampled decoding (T>0) — the load-bearing positive that re-opens option B's *downstream-quality* question (see theme (4)). **Reseated denken → #620 (carry exactly that matched-arm protocol to the MTP candidate + GPQA-D — the binding eval — to decide whether option B's downstream quality = its own AR int4 baseline).**

Reseat lawine → #615 (the foundational follow-up this finding forces: does dev307 *also* distort the **accuracy** evals — MMLU-Pro/GPQA-D/GSM8K/AIME — the way it distorts PPL/decode? if so, every A/B/C quality verdict is scored on the wrong denominator). Relayed the dev307-invalidity consequence to the two live cards it touches: **wirbel #607** (spec verify-path identity census → must run on 0.22.0) + **stark #613** (option-C PPL≤2.42 gate → must be taken on 0.22.0; dev307's +30% would false-FAIL a good sparse config). Roster 8/8, 0 idle. **NO FIRE** (operative submission remains int4_g128_lmhead @ 126.378).

**Theme — the measurement substrate under the entire quality programme just got hardened, and it cleaved into a clean fleet rule plus one decisive open question.** lawine #606 set out to restore the deterministic local greedy-identity gate (broken since the dev307 nondeterminism, lawine #601) and, in doing so, ran a 3-way parity sweep (pinned 0.22.0 vs dev307-local vs the official anchor) that produced the load-bearing result of the cycle: **the dev307 local serve venv is numerically a different model than the 0.22.0 submission stack.** On the *dense shipped int4 checkpoint*, dev307 reads PPL **2.6264 (+30.1%, above the 2.42 cap)** while pinned 0.22.0 reads **2.0188** (= the official anchor 2.019 to 0.01%), and dev307's decode diverges **100% (0/128)** from 0.22.0. The fix is the version pin, not flag-hardening — the candidate determinism flags (`combo_kernels`/`benchmark_combo_kernel`/`enable_flashinfer_autotune`) are identical (all True) in BOTH engine configs, so the regression is intrinsic to dev307's kernel build. **The fleet rule (now load-bearing): all local PPL / greedy-identity / spec-verify-identity work runs on the pinned 0.22.0 venv; dev307 is acceptable only for TPS-magnitude screening (TPS is build-robust, ±0.5% on either, no τ correction in the M=1 non-spec regime).** And the question this forces — the reason I reseated lawine straight onto #615 — is that *every accuracy eval in this programme runs on dev307* (because #547 found "0.22.0 craters MMLU"). #606 and #547 are in tension: if dev307 distorts accuracy the way it distorts PPL/decode, then ubel #590's CI-certs, stark #605's option-B-dead GPQA 0.4141, kanna #610's AR cross-check, fern #612's option-B retry, and the bars themselves are all scored on the wrong stack. Either answer is decisive and cheap.

### lawine #606 — BANKED+MERGED — deterministic local gate RESTORED on pinned vLLM 0.22.0 + dev307-is-not-a-faithful-proxy parity headline

| metric | 0.22.0 (pinned, = submission stack) | dev307-local | official anchor |
|---|---|---|---|
| M=1 wall-TPS | 126.912 / 126.988 | 127.066 / 127.098 | **126.378** |
| PPL | **2.0188** (= official to 0.01%) | **2.6264 (+30.1%)** | 2.019 |
| cross-start greedy-identity | **0/128 (reliable)** | bimodal: 0/128 *or* ~112/128 | 128/128 VALID |
| dev307 decode vs 0.22.0 | — | **0/128 (100% divergent)** | — |

- **Deliverable (1) — gate RESTORED.** Cross-start `ref_v22` vs `ref2_v22` (two separate server processes, byte-identical canonical command) → official `check_greedy_identity.py` = **GREEDY_IDENTICAL, 128/128 prompts, 0/65536 tokens divergent.** 0.22.0 lands on one stable decode attractor every start → the gate is trustworthy again. **The version pin is the proven fix, NOT flag-hardening:** `combo_kernels`/`benchmark_combo_kernel`/`enable_flashinfer_autotune` are identical (all True) in BOTH the deterministic 0.22.0 and the non-deterministic dev307 engine configs (same TRITON_ATTN, Marlin kernel, FULL_AND_PIECEWISE cudagraph, bf16, 9.85 GiB weights + 7.61 GiB KV) → the regression is intrinsic to dev307's kernel build. Recipe: `uv pip install vllm==0.22.0 transformers==5.9.0` + `'fastapi>=0.115,<0.116'`; shipped canonical serve flags unchanged; local-only `CUDA_VISIBLE_DEVICES=0`, `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0` (NOT baked into the submission).
- **★★★ Deliverable (2) — the headline: dev307 is NOT a faithful numerical proxy for the 0.22.0 submission stack.** PPL is build-sensitive and dev307 is numerically WRONG: 2.6264 is +30% over the true 2.019, **bit-identical across two attractor-B draws** (systematic, not noise) and above the 2.42 cap for a checkpoint that really sits at 2.019; dev307 decode diverges 100% from 0.22.0. dev307 cross-start is **BIMODAL** (discrete attractors, timing-selected → a one-shot 0/128 draw can *false-pass* (same attractor) OR *false-fail* (cross-attractor); sharpens lawine #601's ~112/128). **TPS is the one robust axis** — both builds within +0.5% of official 126.378, the M=1 non-spec `wall_tps` is a ~1:1 official proxy (NOT the #267 spec-regime τ=1.0352, which overshoots ~+4% here) → fern's spec 427.7 is trustworthy for *magnitude* regardless of which build measured it.
- **Fleet consequence (load-bearing): all local PPL / greedy-identity / spec-verify-identity work must run on the pinned 0.22.0 venv; dev307 is acceptable only for TPS-magnitude screening.** Logged keys: `ppl_v0220`=2.0188, `ppl_dev307`=2.6264, `ppl_dev307_vs_v0220_pct`=+30.097%, `v0220_crossstart_tokens_divergent`=0, `dev307_vs_v0220_decode_identical`=0, `dev307_crossstart_sameattractor_identical`=128, `dev307_crossstart_crossattractor_identical`=16.

## 2026-06-17 ~19:10Z — Cycle-58AH: two terminals bank — stark #611 (option-C feasibility map → 2:4-sparse W4A16 is the ONLY sub-int4 lever that could beat 126.378 on this A10G; everything else dead) + ubel #590 (base_fullhead quality CI-CERTIFIED under the sampled protocol, AND the headline cross-experiment unlock: MMLU-Pro's apparent fail was a `max_tokens=2048` OUTPUT-truncation artifact — empirical proof on THIS stack of the exact mechanism fern #612 is testing). Reseat stark → #613 (option-C build: dev307 kernel grep → build+measure 2:4-sparse W4A16) + ubel → #614 (is the 0.471 GPQA bar itself truncation-clean + regime-consistent?). Relayed #590's truncation proof to fern #612 with a sharpened budget (≥4096, log `finish_reason=length` at both caps). Roster 8/8, 0 idle. **NO FIRE** (operative submission remains int4_g128_lmhead @ 126.378; option C is non-byte-identical → any gate-clearing config SURFACES to the human for the relax-#319 call, never auto-fires).

**Theme — the generation-budget OUTPUT-truncation class is now EMPIRICALLY REAL on this exact stack, and option C narrows to a single decisive grep.** ubel #590 ran the quality-gate CI-lb harness on base_fullhead and, beyond cleanly CI-certifying it (`all3_ci_lb_clear_bars=TRUE` under the mandated lewtun#31 sampled ≥5-seed protocol), surfaced the load-bearing finding: under T=1.0 sampling, ~12.4% of MMLU-Pro items hit `stop_reason=max_tokens` at a 2048 output cap and **244/245 of those scored *wrong*** purely because the `ANSWER:` line never emitted before the cutoff — not a quality loss, a generation-budget artifact. De-biasing at `max_tokens=4096` (byte-identical prompts, same sampling seed) recovered MMLU-Pro 0.616→0.6695. This is **direct, on-this-exact-stack proof of the mechanism fern #612 is running right now** (does a generous gen budget lift option B's GPQA 0.4141?). GPQA CoT is *longer* than MMLU-Pro's, so the effect could be larger there — I relayed this to #612 and sharpened its budget (ubel found 2048 too small AND 4096 only *partly* sufficient: 408/991 truncated recovered at 4096, the rest need >4096). Meanwhile stark #611's option-C feasibility map narrowed the "fewer bytes/token" lever to exactly one live candidate: **2:4-structured-sparse W4A16 (sparse-Marlin)** — every other sub-int4/mixed format is dead for a concrete hardware reason. The single blocking unknown is a 5-min grep (does dev307's vLLM still carry the sparse-Marlin kernel?), so I reseated stark straight onto the build (#613). And because #590 proved the bars themselves may be measured against the wrong (greedy-base, short-CoT) denominator while configs are scored under the sampled protocol, I spun ubel a new orthogonal validity card (#614) to certify the keystone GPQA-D bar is truncation-clean. Roster 8/8, 0 idle. **NO FIRE.**

### stark #611 — BANKED+MERGED — option-C feasibility map: 2:4-sparse W4A16 (sparse-Marlin) is the ONLY sub-int4/mixed-precision format that could beat 126.378 on this A10G (sm_86); everything else closed
- stark; **analysis-only, NO W&B run by design** (`official_tps=0`, `analysis_only=true`, `wandb_run_ids:[]` — a kernel/literature desk-research card; the deliverable IS the scoping, nothing measured to W&B-verify). Bank comment issuecomment-4734261525, merge brought the #611 feasibility map + recipe + summary table onto the advisor branch.

| lever | loads on sm_86? | why |
|---|---|---|
| **(a) 2:4-sparse W4A16** | **CONDITIONAL** | kernel (`gptq_marlin_24_gemm` + `CompressedTensors24`) is dev307-era; ~32% HBM read cut → **~186 TPS theoretical ceiling**; PPL risk LOW (+0.05–0.15 ≪ the 0.40 gap to 2.42); quality risk concentrated at **GPQA-D** (already the binding constraint) |
| (b) mixed w4/w3 | NO | no 3-bit Marlin on Ampere (machete needs Hopper sm_90+) |
| (c) uniform w3a16 | NO | same kernel gap + quality-catastrophic |
| NVFP4 / MXFP4 | NO | Blackwell sm_100+ only |
| HQQ-4bit | loads, **no gain** | same bytes/token as GPTQ-W4A16 |
| HQQ-3bit / AWQ-2bit / QuIP# / AQLM | NO / catastrophic | no kernel, or PPL ≫ 2.42 |

- **VERDICT — narrows option C to one decisive cheap step, does NOT close it.** The single blocking unknown: dev307's vLLM vintage vs the sparse-Marlin kernel lifecycle (added vLLM PR#4790/#5435 mid-2024; sparse support later *removed* from llm-compressor PR#36799 — dev307 decides which side of that removal we sit on). Resolvable by a **5-min grep, not a build.** With the AR frontier pinned at the HBM ceiling (126.378, lawine #601), the alternate-engine axis closed, and spec (option B) pending fern #612, reading fewer bytes/token is the only remaining physics to beat 126.378 — exactly the human's "next +10 over 126" incremental-cadence ask (#481).

### Reseat (Cycle-58AH) — stark → #613 `optionc-sparse24-w4a16` (grep, then build+measure)
- **stark → #613** — Phase-0 dev307 kernel grep (`CompressedTensors24`/`gptq_marlin_24_gemm`): if ABSENT → terminal finding, option C **CLOSED**, 126.378 confirmed as the quality-safe AR ceiling, stop (don't build). If PRESENT → Phase-0b disk preflight (≥170 GB) → build 2:4-sparse W4A16 from `gemma-4-E4B-it-qat-q4_0-unquantized` via the #611 two-stage recipe (`SparseGPTModifier(sparsity=0.5, 2:4, ignore=lm_head/self_attn/embed)` → `GPTQModifier(W4A16, ignore=lm_head)`, open_platypus, 512 calib) → Stage-1 serve + `bytes_read_per_token` + `proxy_tps` (measured exactly as the 126.378 baseline) + PPL ≤ 2.42 → Stage-2 4-eval gate ONLY if proxy_tps>126.378 AND PPL≤2.42, with all guards (#541 min_tokens=8, #598 max-model-len 6144, #31 sampling) **AND CRITICAL `max_tokens≥4096` + `finish_reason=length` logging per ubel #590** (do not let a truncation artifact masquerade as a quality loss; GPQA-D is the likely wall). Mitigations in pocket: half-sparse (gate/up_proj sparse, down_proj dense, ~150 TPS); calibration-aware prune. dev307 (#547), `--wandb_group optionc-sparse24-w4a16`, LOCAL/analysis_only/official_tps=0/NO FIRE — non-byte-identical → SURFACE.

### ubel #590 — BANKED+MERGED — base_fullhead quality CI-CERTIFIED (`all3_ci_lb_clear_bars=TRUE`, sampled protocol) + the MMLU-Pro `max_tokens=2048` OUTPUT-truncation artifact (empirical proof of the fern #612 mechanism on this stack)
- ubel; run [`24317e29`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/24317e29), group `quality-gates-ci`, `finished`. **W&B-verified — every number exact to reported precision, finished clean, `official_tps=0`/`analysis_only=true` both confirmed in config, no submission fired.** Bank comment issuecomment-4734268016, merge brought the reusable `debias_mmlu.py` / `aggregate_ci.py` CI harness onto the advisor branch.

| gate | protocol | 95% CI-lb | bar | margin |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| GSM8K | sampled, 5 seeds, n=500 | **0.872** | 0.807 | +32.5 q |
| MMLU-Pro (de-biased) | sampled, 5 seeds, N=2000 | **0.6511** | 0.605 | +92.2 q |
| AIME (sampled) | k=5×5 seeds, n=60 | **0.172** | 0.090 | +4.9 q; maj@25 Wilson-lb 0.256 |

- **VERDICT — `all3_ci_lb_clear_bars = TRUE`** under the mandated lewtun#31 sampled ≥5-seed protocol: base_fullhead's quality gates hold on CI *lower bounds*, not just point estimates. base_fullhead quality is CI-certified.
- **★★★ FINDING 1 (the cross-experiment unlock) — MMLU-Pro's apparent fail was a `max_tokens=2048` OUTPUT-truncation artifact, not a quality margin.** ~12.4% of items hit `stop_reason=max_tokens` at the 2048 cap; **244/245 of those scored wrong** (under T=1.0 the CoT runs long, the `ANSWER: X` line never emits before cutoff). Non-truncated items score ~0.70; the truncated ~12% dragged the measured mean to 0.616 (CI-lb 0.5999, a hair under 0.605 → looked like a FAIL). De-biased at `max_tokens=4096` (clean A/B: same sampling seed, byte-identical prompts, `prompt_sha` 0-mismatch, seed-2 fresh-N cross-check 0.667) → **0.6695, CI-lb 0.6511 ≫ 0.605.** ubel found 2048 too small AND **4096 only *partly* sufficient** (408/991 truncated items recovered at 4096; the rest need >4096). **This is direct, on-this-exact-stack proof of the mechanism fern #612 is running** (does a generous gen budget lift option B's GPQA 0.4141?) — GPQA CoT is longer than MMLU-Pro's, so the effect could be larger → relayed to #612 (issuecomment-4734305589) with the sharpened ≥4096 budget + dual-cap `finish_reason` logging.
- **FINDING 2 — AIME count-tightness is a measurement-power artifact, not quality.** Held to the bar's native greedy maj@1 regime, base_fullhead is 7/60=0.1167 with 95% CI-lb 0.050 (< 0.090) — n=60 can't resolve "≥5.4/60" from "<5.4/60". Under the sampled protocol AIME clears comfortably (CI-lb 0.172). Capability fine; single-greedy-at-n=60 *measurement* underpowered.
- **Honest caveat carried forward:** secondary verdict `all3_ci_lb_clear_bars_aime_greedy_regime = FALSE` (greedy-AIME alone, CI-lb 0.050). base_fullhead quality is CI-certified **under the sampled protocol**; the greedy-regime AIME gate at n=60 is not certifiable from one pass (fix = expand the AIME set or adopt sampled maj@k as the official AIME gate). Independent of base_fullhead's *speed* standing (separate lane).

### Reseat (Cycle-58AH) — ubel → #614 `gpqa-bar-validity` (is the keystone 0.471 GPQA bar truncation-clean + regime-consistent?)
- **ubel → #614** — #590 proved sampled-protocol output-truncation; the four ≥90% bars are `0.9×greedy-base` but every config is now measured under the **sampled** protocol (longer CoT, truncation-prone) → a regime+truncation mismatch may mis-calibrate every quality verdict, GPQA-D especially (the binding constraint that killed option B, knife-edges option A, and is option C's likely wall). ### lawine #601 — BANKED+MERGED — M=1 decode is HBM-bandwidth-bound (76.2% int4 Marlin reads, 9.85 GiB/token, attention 5.6%, 0.69% arm spread) → the LAST strict-#319 AR lever is a NEGATIVE; FlashInfer structurally dead on Gemma4

With body + lm_head (#593) both closed and spec closed under #319 (fern #597), the strict-#319 AR +10 lives entirely in lawine #601 (attention/graph/scheduler) + fern #604 (host-tail). stark reseated OFF the closed body lane onto the highest-EV open question: the **missing #481 A/B decision evidence** — the 4-eval ≥90% quality panel on the int4+MTP spec config (#605), which either makes option B fireable or collapses the A/B decision to A. ### stark #602 — BANKED+MERGED — int4 BODY GEMV is bandwidth-bound at M=1 (84.2% read-peak), 0.0 byte-identical headroom, Marlin M=1 is the FIXED reference → AR +10 hands to lawine #601 + fern #604

Per the #481 trigger (`identity==1.0` **AND** TPS>126.378) this is **NOT** an autonomous fire — it is a human trade-off, so the advisor **SURFACED** it on #481 rather than firing. The mechanism is complete and generalizes: vLLM's spec-verify IS exact-greedy (`rejection_greedy_sample_kernel`, accept iff == target argmax), so the breaker is one level down — the **int4 W4A16 Marlin GEMM's M-dependent tiling, uncovered by `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1`** (M=8 verify ≠ M=1 AR → target argmax flips → drafted tokens cascade). No flag fixes it; **any spec method with an M>1 verify hits the same wall on an int4 body** (denken #600 confirming body-vs-head now — fern's read: body alone breaks it → entire spec-dec fire lane closed under #319). The live +10 hunt is therefore entirely **AR-frame**: stark #602 (body GEMV) + lawine #601 (attention/graph) + fern's reseat #604 (logits→token tail). Roster 8/8, 0 idle. **NO FIRE** (operative submission remains int4_g128_lmhead @ 126.378, strict #319, quality-GREEN).

### fern #597 — BANKED+MERGED — int4_g128_lmhead + MTP-K7: SURFACE-TO-HUMAN (strict #319 FAILS 0.3125, TPS clears 427.7) — spec-dec fire lane structurally closed under strict identity

Merge `c99abbc` (#597 squash). Bank comment issuecomment-4732847198.
- **VERDICT — SURFACE-TO-HUMAN, NO autonomous fire.** `served_freerun_seq_exact=0.3125` → strict #319 greedy gate FAILS on the exact int4_g128_lmhead substrate; `official_proxy_tps=427.7` clears 126.378 by ~3.4×. #481 trigger requires identity==1.0 AND TPS>126.378 → identity fails hard → NOT a fire. The advisor surfaced the speed-vs-gate trade to the human (#481), did not fire.
- **Mechanism (complete, generalizes) — structural int4-Marlin M-dependence, NOT a fixable flag.** (1) vLLM 0.22.0 `rejection_greedy_sample_kernel` IS exact-greedy (`mtp_verify_is_exact_greedy=TRUE`) → divergence is NOT rejection-sampling/acceptance-rule. (2) Breaker = the int4 W4A16 Marlin GEMM has **M-dependent tiling** and `VLLM_BATCH_INVARIANT=1` does NOT (and in this build cannot) cover `gptq_marlin_gemm`; verify M=K+1=8 ≠ AR M=1 → different logits → target argmax flips → cascade. 46% of tokens, onset early+wide = structural, **NOT ≤4-ULP near-tie noise**. (3) selfdet anchor: spec-OFF AR reference cross-server seq_exact=1.0 (clean gate floor reproduces run-to-run) yet spec-ON still 0.3125 → real property of speculation, not measurement noise.
- **Determinism≠identity correction (load-bearing; advisor owns the bad framing).** At 15:36Z the advisor told the human fern's identity leg was "landing GREEN" off denken #596's warm determinism=1.0. fern correctly refuted: determinism ("same stack reproduces run-to-run") ≠ greedy-identity ("spec-ON == M=1 AR"). The selfdet probe CONFIRMS determinism (1.0) and STILL fails the gate (0.3125) — the M=8 verify forward is systematically (reproducibly) different from the M=1 AR forward. **Warm determinism was never a valid proxy for the #319/#566 gate.** Corrected with the human in the same #481 comment.
- **OOM control = real finding.** A fully-bf16 control OOM'd during KV-profiling (bf16 weights 15.24 GiB vs int4 9.99 GiB + M=8 verify activations > 23 GiB A10G) → **int4 is precisely what makes MTP spec-dec FIT on this GPU, and the same int4 Marlin kernel is what breaks byte-identity.** Can't have the fast spec lane and strict #319 at once on this hardware with a full-bf16 body → exactly why denken #600's base_mtp (int4 body + bf16 head) is the right isolation.
- **Decision impact:** spec-dec is fast but cannot be strict-#319 byte-exact on int4/A10G (structural). The whole +10 hunt is now AR-frame (3 orthogonal levers: stark #602 body-GEMV, lawine #601 attention/graph, fern #604 logits→token tail). Quality panel NOT run (the "by construction" shortcut is void once identity≠1.0) — if the human elects option B (#481), the next step is the 4-eval ≥90% panel on int4+MTP, then fire.

## 2026-06-17 ~15:00Z — Cycle-58X: the single load-bearing quality soft spot is CHARACTERIZED — base_fullhead's GPQA-Diamond gate margin PASSES on the point but is NOT CI-robust (un-tightenable on the n=198 Diamond ceiling); it is an INSTRUMENT limit, not a regression, and it applies head-invariantly to the whole int4-body family (shipped 126.38 + fern #597)

**Theme — land #589 closes the GPQA leg of the quality gate with the honest two-readings answer the #587 capstone flagged.** The terminal card (W&B-verified `avij33aw`, NaN-clean, K=10 sampling seeds under the lewtun-#31 protocol) resolves the +0.009 GPQA-Diamond knife-edge that fern #587 named as the one soft spot in the convergence claim: **(1) the point estimate is NOT violated** — 10-seed sampled mean **0.4869** (+0.0159 over the ≥0.471 bar) is *above* the greedy anchor 0.4798, so sampling does not lower the central estimate and **there is no evidence base_fullhead is a regression** vs the denominator. **(2) the margin is NOT CI-robust** — every 95% lower bound dips below 0.471 (primary clustered-bootstrap 0.4343, worst seed 0.4495, 3/10 seeds below gate). **(3) the structural keeper:** the gate is **un-tightenable on the Diamond instrument** — a Wilson-lb clears 0.471 only at **n≈3758**; GPQA-Diamond is n=198 and the harness exposes no larger GPQA task. So GPQA-Diamond can never supply a 95%-CI-lb gate at ~0.487 true accuracy. **Decision banked:** for the autonomous-fire quality gate, GPQA must be read as a **point/seed-mean gate** (which the int4 body clears), with the CI-fragility now *characterized* rather than lurking. Because the gate metrics are **body-set/head-invariant** (stark #536), this is symmetric across the int4-body family — it covers the shipped `int4_g128_lmhead` @ 126.38 (which the human already accepted as GREEN on the point) AND fern #597's int4_g128+MTP fire candidate; it does **not** single out any config as a regression and is **NOT a fire-blocker**. land reseated onto its own #1 follow-up (#598: larger GPQA instrument + paired no-regression McNemar). Roster 8/8, 0 idle. BASELINE 481.53 untouched, NO FIRE this cycle.

### land #589 — BANKED+MERGED — GPQA-Diamond gate margin: point PASSES (0.4869) but CI-lb FAILS, un-tightenable at n≈3758 → instrument limit, not a regression
- land; run [`avij33aw`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/avij33aw), group `gpqa-ci-tighten`, finished, NaN-clean (W&B-verified: `gpqa_ci_lb_clears_0471=False`, `worst_seed_clears_0471=False`, `gpqa/mean_acc=0.48686`, `min_seed_acc=0.44949`, `bootstrap_cilb_items=0.43434`, `n_for_wilson_cilb_pass_at_point=3758`, `ci_untightenable_on_diamond=True`, `analysis_only=True`, `official_tps=0`, `no_hf_job=True`). base_fullhead config + sampling temp=1.0/top_p=0.95/top_k=64, K=10 seeds confirmed. Tool: `research/validity/base_fullhead_gpqa_ci_tighten/`.
- **VERDICT `gpqa_ci_lb_clears_0471=FALSE`** — but the two honest readings matter: the **point CLEARS** (sampled 0.4869 ≥ greedy 0.4798 ≥ 0.471 bar; sampling does not move the central estimate down → no regression signal), while **all four interval lenses FAIL** (clustered-bootstrap 0.4343, Wilson 0.4162, pooled-Wilson 0.4649, seed-mean-t 0.4705 — the most generous now fails by −0.0005). The +0.0159 cushion is thinner than the n=198 binomial item-sampling noise.
- **Structural finding (durable):** `ci_untightenable_on_diamond=TRUE` — need n≈3758 for a Wilson-lb to clear at the point; Diamond is n=198 and the harness has no larger GPQA task. More decode seeds shrink seed-noise but cannot shrink the binomial item-sampling floor. Reproduces+extends the #564/#574 "198-item ceiling" under the K=10 sampling protocol. `min_tokens=8` was a mechanical no-op here (`empty_rate=0` every seed, confirming kanna #563).

### land #571 — BANKED+MERGED — the BODY leg of the per-component byte-identity census: `body_strict_safe_lever_exists=False` → `any_strict_safe_speed_lever_anywhere=False`
- land; run [`vct3k1vc`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/vct3k1vc), group `body-strict-identity-census`, finished. Applies the #556 per-precision greedy-argmax flip census + served-TPS method to the BODY weights {int4_g32 [served] / int4_g128 / fp8 / int8 / bf16 ref}, head+attention held at the served reference.
- **The only faster body precision breaks identity:** int4_g128 (coarser groups → fewer scales to read) is +6.38 TPS over the served int4_g32 (259.07 vs 252.69) but flips **0.1374** of argmax tokens vs the bf16 reference and is NOT byte-identical to the served int4_g32 → quality-near-neutral but not #319-byte-exact. The served int4_g32 itself flips 0.10902 vs bf16 (its own quantization, the reference the served stack defines). Every finer option (fp8/int8/bf16) is SLOWER. So no body precision is both faster AND byte-identical → `body_strict_safe_lever_exists=False`, `body_flip_is_near_tie_concentrated=True`.
- **Census complete:** HEAD GEMV (#556/#555) No / ATTENTION SDPA (#562) No / BODY int4 (#571) No → `all_three_components_censused=True`, `any_strict_safe_speed_lever_anywhere=False`. Reconciles stark #536's `collapse_locus=BODY` with the legality picture: the body is where coarser quant both speeds up AND breaks identity, which is exactly why a quality-safe ship cannot lean on a faster body precision. The within-stack per-token decode is byte-identity-saturated; the only axis the per-token ceiling does NOT bound is spec-dec.

### ubel #567 — BANKED+MERGED — base_fullhead AIME = 0.1167 (7/60) FAILS the ≥90% bar (0.360), but as an int4-QUANT TAX (0.1167 > plain-int4 0.0667), NOT a speed-stack regression
- ubel; run [`ns5l6i28`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ns5l6i28), group `base-fullhead-aime`, finished; AIME-25 maj@1 greedy, EOS-guard `min_tokens=8` applied (the #541 serving artifact fix), full-60 loader via the wirbel AIME bug-fix (canonical `"2025"` key → `math-ai/aime25`, routing around the `opencompass/AIME2025` HTTP-500 outage).
- **The number and its interpretation:** base_fullhead (stock base-int4 BODY + full native 262k head, the quality-safe config) scores **0.1167** (7/60). The ≥90%-of-unquantized gate bar is 0.360 (0.9×0.400) → FAIL. But base_fullhead AIME *improves 1.75×* over plain int4 base (0.0667) — the loss is the int4 quantization of the body, not the full-head speed stack. **Implication:** if the gate denominator is the UNQUANTIZED base's AIME (0.400 cited), the bar may be structurally unpassable by ANY int4 config — the int4-QAT checkpoint we are required to serve cannot recover unquantized-grade AIME. This is why ubel reseats to **#580** (ground the unquantized-base AIME denominator directly) — to decide whether the bar is "we regressed" vs "the int4 floor is below the bar regardless of speed stack". (GSM8K ✓ #541, MMLU-Pro ✓ #538, GPQA ✓ #574, sampling-protocol ✓ #563 all clear; AIME is the lone gate-failing axis and the cause is now pinned to int4, not the speed work.)

### stark #577 — BANKED+MERGED — spec-dec worst-case/dispersion: worst-case stratum net-TPS 185.1 ≪ ship → `specdec_worstcase_clears_ship=False` (workload-FRAGILE)
- stark; run [`q5631wt0`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/q5631wt0), group `base-fullhead-specdec-dispersion`, finished; serves base_fullhead + spec-dec greedy across 3 workload strata, measures per-stratum acceptance + net-TPS, reports dispersion + worst-case (the conservative speed-gate input).
- **Per-stratum (accept-rate / net-TPS):** EASY 0.257 / **185.1**, MIX 0.400 / 250.3, HARD 0.509 / **309.9**. Either way the dispersion is large (~1.67× spread) and the WORST-CASE net-TPS is 185.1, far below the ship even before σ_hw → `specdec_worstcase_net_tps≈185.1`, worst-case − σ_hw ≪ 375.857 → `specdec_worstcase_clears_ship=0`; `self_det=0.875`.
- **The conservative read:** wirbel #575's asymptote (~11817) and stark #577's worst-case (185.1) tell OPPOSITE stories. A MEAN spec-dec number could clear the ship on a favorable workload, but the gate is certified on hard reasoning and the served TPS benchmark is single-stream worst-case-exposed — so the conservative speed gate uses the tail → spec-dec is WORKLOAD-FRAGILE → NO-FIRE on the speed side independent of the #319-identity question (still open at denken #576). ### denken #569 — BANKED+MERGED (Morgan, 717466d) — decode-overhead floor MEASURED: 311.27 reproduced, CUDA-graph lever irreducible
- denken; run [`ehzbiwhx`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ehzbiwhx), group `decode-overhead-floor-reducibility`, finished; terminal `SENPAI-RESULT` (`fixed_overhead_frac=0.0387`, `free_head_floor_reproduced_tps=311.27`, `analysis_only=true`, `official_tps=0`, 14/14 self-tests). Morgan posted the bank comment + squash-merged (his ADVISOR-prefixed comment 07:45:56Z; merged 07:46:13Z) — I defer to his bank under the shared advisor identity.
- **Decode-step breakdown (M=1, AR, CUDA-graph ON, served-faithful):** body GEMVs **65.3%** (int4 Marlin w4a16 ×42, HBM-bound) · head GEMV **26.9%** (bf16 262k tied lm_head, HBM-bound) · attention **3.9%** (Triton unified_attention, 35×hs256+7×hs512) · fixed overhead **3.9%** (sampling 14.8 + norm/act 172.1 + other-device 170.2 + **host/launch 41.5** us). `sum_closure_err=0.0`. The two BW-bound matmul reads = 92.2%, both byte-locked by #555.
- **CUDA-graph reducibility lever — tested, IRREDUCIBLE in the served config:** graph ON 97.01 TPS vs OFF 21.23 TPS (+357% AR, math-preserving — `cudagraph_identity_preserved=true`, byte-identical greedy IDs) — BUT graphs are already fully pulled in the served path (`served_cudagraph_further_headroom_tps=0.0`); with graphs ON the host overhead it targets is `host_overhead_frac_graph=0.0040` (0.4%), and fully eliminating it lifts AR TPS to only `ar_tps_if_host_eliminated=97.40` (+0.4%). This EMPIRICALLY confirms #554's `attackable_frac=0` assertion *in the served config* and pins down *why* (graphs on + host slice is 0.4%).
- **Found+fixed a profiler double-count bug** (flat `key_averages` attributes the same `self_device_time_total` to both the CPU op and its child kernel + bogus `Activity Buffer Request` rows; eager body inflated 12680→6316 us ≈ graph's 6728 us after dedup). Fix: sum self-device over `DeviceType.CUDA` events only. Faithfulness preserved (excluded CPU-op rows retained in artifact).
- **Net — within-stack speed door shut from both ends.** denken's own recommendation: treat 311.25 as the MEASURED loosest NO-FIRE ceiling; the only remaining lever bypassing the per-token ceiling is spec-dec (amortizes all components across accepted tokens) — and that axis is at the 3-student cap (#572/#573/#575).

### fern #566 — BANKED+MERGED (0376e59) — served candidate-verify: SPEED 299.28 validated, IDENTITY 0.9941/step → #319 FAILS served (quality-neutral)
- fern; run [`ix0oap4a`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ix0oap4a), group `served-cv-realize`, finished; **W&B-verified all headline fields MATCH** (`cv_served_tps_measured=299.28`, `cv_served_argmax_identity_rate=0.4494`, per-step `cv_audit_per_step_identity_rate=0.99406`, `cv_served_sequence_exact_rate=0.140625`, `cv_served_tps_is_measured=True`, `clears_surgical_357_ship=False`, `cv_served_tps_on_252_anchor=287.01`, `cv_realized_vs_reproj560_delta=−4.36`, `analysis_only=True`, `official_tps=0`, peak 21.68 GB). One non-blocking gap (`candidate_verify_can_fire` absent from summary; gate unambiguous via `clears_surgical_357_ship=False` + `official_tps=0`).
- **The integration (real Marlin uint4b8 inside the real sampler, no served-file change):** `Gemma4ForCausalLM.compute_logits` replaced at runtime via a default-off `sitecustomize`/`CV_HEAD` meta-path probe on PYTHONPATH; int4-nominator GEMV (0.346 GB read) → top-8 → gather 8 bf16 rows → exact bf16 verify → scatter into [M,vocab] at TRUE vocab indices; server's PyTorch-native `torch.argmax` (lowest-index, `VLLM_USE_FLASHINFER_SAMPLER=0`) argmaxes. Confirmed live (`m_hist={1:14, 8:1218}` → int4 path runs at MTP M=8 verify granularity, not CUDA-graph-bypassed).
- **Mechanism of the per-step misses (why K-sweep can't fix it):** every miss is a bf16 TIE — e.g. `ref_logit[108]==ref_logit[63311]==23.875`; the reference picks 108 (lowest index) but the int4 nominator mis-ranks 108 OUT of the shortlist by >32 positions, so the bf16 verify (re-ranks WITHIN shortlist only) never recovers it. The chosen co-winner has the IDENTICAL logit → zero quality cost, but byte-divergent. The vocab-index tie-break was correctly baked in (necessary, NOT sufficient — the miss is a ranking error below it). K=8/16/32 byte-identical (58/9758 each).
- **Why it STRENGTHENS NO-FIRE:** `candidate_verify_can_fire=FALSE` now empirical on BOTH gates (speed 299<376 AND #319-fail). The conjunction is unchanged (cv failed speed regardless), but the identity-safe head ceiling drops from a hoped 291 to **252.31** → identity-safe gap-to-ship widens to 123.5. Three-method convergence: land #556 (offline census) + denken #555 (BW probe) + fern #566 (served realization) all → no #319-safe head lever beats ~252. The candidate-verify "exception" was an offline-check artifact, now closed served.
- **For-the-record (ruled non-load-bearing):** IF the contract were quality-only (PPL/maj@1) not strict byte-exact, candidate-verify passes + ships +36 — but #319 strict is the LIVE launch contract (human #319). So the cv NO-FIRE is an identity-contract failure, not a quality one (and it's 299<376 anyway). Methodology credit: fern UPHELD the #561 "no asserted-but-unchecked claim" standard by refuting a by-construction 1.0 with an empirical served check — relayed to lawine #572 as the cautionary precedent for her spec-dec identity check.

- denken; run [`am7kltht`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/am7kltht), group `attention-byte-identical-ceiling`, finished, NaN-clean, `self_test_passes=True`. `decode_step_byte_identical_lever_exists=False`, `attn_byte_identical_lever_is_green=False`, `attn_byte_identical_wall_GBs=84.28`. The 3 genuinely-faster attention configs (hs256 seg32 −21.8%, hs256 tile_alt −6.6%, hs512 seg32 −15.8%) ALL have `bitwise_rate=0.0` — faster ⟺ reorders the softmax/PV reduction ⟺ not bit-identical (the #555 mechanism reproduced in attention). The only bit-identical config (served 3D split-softmax) is not faster.
- **Two validity legs that make it trustworthy, not asserted:** (1) faithfulness anchor `rerun_equals_live=1` (maxΔ=0.0) on BOTH layer classes — the Stage-2 reference IS served truth bit-for-bit (caught + fixed 2 harness bugs first: a non-contiguous reshape zeroing the rebuilt cache, a tile-override leak); (2) the #555 trap reproduced — `argmax_identity_rate=1.0` for EVERY divergent config → a finite-sample argmax census would have falsely greenlit seg32/tile_alt/2D/SDPA/fp32; the bitwise probe correctly rejects all. This is exactly why #319 needs the bitwise gate, not an argmax census.
- **Architecture pinned:** Gemma4ForConditionalGeneration, 42 layers all TritonAttentionImpl (vLLM forces TRITON_ATTN for het head_dim 256/512 on sm_86), 35× hs256 sliding (window 512) + 7× hs512 global, both on 3D split-softmax at M=1; attention is overhead-bound at M=1 (14–48% of peak, well below the head's 80.6%) — consistent with #554's launch floor. **Census complete:** HEAD GEMV (#555) No / BODY int4 Marlin (#550) No / ATTENTION SDPA (#562) No → upgrades #561's "no byte-identical lever anywhere in the decode step" from assertion to MEASURED on all three components.

### fern #560 — BANKED+MERGED (3b85e85) — candidate-verify served realization: +39.1→291.36 quality-safe TPS, identity 1.0 IFF vocab-index tie-break → `candidate_verify_can_fire=FALSE` MEASURED on the real Marlin kernel
- fern; run [`ufv4nk21`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/ufv4nk21), group `candidate-verify-realize`, finished; **W&B-verified ALL load-bearing fields MATCH** (`cv_realized_quality_safe_tps=291.36`, `argmax_identity_rate=1.0`, `cv_int4_nominator_bw_GBs=510.44` = 85.07% peak, `cv_realized_tps_gain=39.05`, implied anchor 252.31, `cv_served_tps_is_measured=False`, `analysis_only=True`, `official_tps=0`, `self_det=1.0`, peak GPU 12.09 GiB, state `finished`).
- **The hypothesized downside is REFUTED — that's the finding.** The one unmeasured kernel (small-M int4-nominator Marlin GEMV) runs at **510.4 GB/s = 85.1% of A10G peak at M=1**, *above* denken #550's bf16 head GEMV (482.9 / 80.5%): the worry "small-M int4 Marlin less BW-efficient → realized gain < +40" does NOT happen. GEMV-only speedup **3.87× ≈ byte ratio 1.342/0.346 = 3.88×** → the entire saving is bandwidth, no small-M dequant tax. Re-projects to **+39.1 → 291.4** on wirbel #553's 252.31 anchor — inside #549's [+28,+44] band, **84.5 below the 375.857 ship**. Independently confirms lawine #544's +38.3→292.1 cross-check: the head ceiling holds ~292, it does not drop.
- **The HARD-gate served-realization spec (the substantive catch beyond #549's offline proof):** realized-path argmax identity = 1.0 over 60k positions **IFF** the verify re-rank breaks exact-bf16-logit ties by **lowest vocab index** (vLLM's `torch.argmax` first-index convention). The naive shortlist-position tie-break gives 0.99545 (272/60k spurious misses) — and fern proved this is a pure tie-break artifact NOT precision (gathering the *exact* served bf16 logits still yields 0.99545 under position tie-break, snaps to 1.0 under vocab-index). So #549's offline `miss@8=0` containment is necessary-but-not-sufficient; a served realization must additionally replicate the server's tie-break or it silently leaks ~0.45% greedy mismatches.
- **Confirms the capstone:** candidate-verify is the ONE strict-#319-identity head-speed lever (head-width LOSES tokens per kanna #547; lossless-prune is +0 per land #552; precision is the parallel lever lawine #544/wirbel #553 owns). It realizes to 291.4 — below even the 311.25 magically-free ceiling, 84.5 below the ship = `candidate_verify_can_fire=FALSE` (capstone #561), now MEASURED on the real Marlin uint4b8 kernel (not the quantize→dequantize sim). `cv_served_tps_is_measured=False` is the one honest residual (Stage-1 microbench + Stage-2 re-projection; full vLLM decode-loop integration time-boxed away).
- **Forward hook → fern #566** (`served-cv-realize`): wire the candidate-verify head into the live vLLM sampler (int4-Marlin nominator → top-8 gather → exact bf16 verify with the mandatory vocab-index tie-break) and measure served e2e single-stream TPS + live-sampler argmax identity — converts `cv_served_tps_is_measured` to a real number (predicted ~291 < 375.857, identity 1.0). Time-boxed: fallback = largest-coverage served decode-loop microbench. Disjoint from wirbel #553 (precision lever served). LOCAL/analysis_only/NO FIRE.

### denken #555 — BANKED+MERGED (02f8144) — the byte-rate end: byte-identical head-read wall = served ~483.4 GB/s, NO sliver (6th convergent lens)

- **The correction to #550's ASSUMPTION:** the RAW achievable head read is MEASURED **543.9 GB/s = 90.6% of peak** (not the 80-85% #550 guessed) — but every config that reads faster than served retiles/reorders the K=2560 reduction (land #506), changes bf16 rounding, and breaks byte-identity. So the byte-IDENTICAL wall stays at served ~483.4 GB/s and `identity_safe_head_tps = 252.36 ≈ BFH 252.31` → **zero free head TPS**; #544's 328.9 "magically-free head" stays optimistic from the byte-rate side (two-ended 255.1 strict).

Only the 4 configs at served ~483 GB/s are bitwise-identical (`max_abs_logit_diff=0.0`). **Materially-faster ⟺ reduction-order-changed ⟺ not byte-identical** — the byte-identity tax in its purest measured form.
- **Contract-fork RULED:** do NOT pursue the 521 GB/s argmax-risky read — it contradicts the #319 STRICT byte-identical greedy-token contract (the LIVE LAUNCH CONTRACT). A 768-input argmax pass is necessary-not-sufficient; relaxing byte-identity to argmax-identity is a contract change only the human team can authorize. denken correctly flagged-and-declined.

**Theme — at strict identity the full head is IRREDUCIBLE by pruning, and the central program ceiling (~292) now rests on an UNMEASURED assumption land #556 will settle.** land #552 closes the "smaller EXACT head" lever with a clean strict-tier NO-GO: a provably-lossless prune keeps **all 262,144 rows** → **+0 TPS** (`provably_unreachable_rows=0`, `provable_prune_tps_gain=0.0`, `beats_lawine_38=False`). The reason is **geometric, not a tooling limit**: after Gemma's final RMSNorm the attainable hidden state spans a **CENTERED, full-dimensional ellipsoid** (γ=`norm.weight`, all `1+γ_i>0`), and *centered* ⇒ for any attainable `h`, `−h` is also attainable ⇒ no kept row can pairwise-dominate a nonzero-norm row ⇒ the only provably-never-argmax rows are exact zero-norm rows — and the near-uniform head (row L2 norms tight in **[0.9658, 0.9901]**) has none. Density diagnostic: **245,592 of 245,760 dropped rows (99.93%) are POSITIVELY certified reachable** — dense support, no harvestable tail. ## 2026-06-17 ~02:38Z — Cycle-57U: the speed-frontier verdict QUANTIFIES — lawine #544 BANKED+MERGED (05938ca, the quality-safe ship is structurally capped ~292, can't beat 442) + denken #540 BANKED+MERGED (7af5c0b, fast kernels bit-identical, 10th lens); reseat denken → #550 (faster byte-identical KERNEL) + lawine → #551 (KV-read + fp8-KV — the two last un-capped quality-safe speed levers)

**Theme — the precision lever for the quality-safe ship is now CAPPED with a hard number, and the strategic question sharpens to "is there ANY quality-safe path to the unsafe class."** lawine #544 decomposed the ~100-TPS base_fullhead(252.31)→osoi5(350.76) gap: **82.2% the 262k-head verify-tax + 17.8% the +5 body layers** (MTP K=7 drafter a non-factor, E[T] 3.819 vs 3.804). The only identity-preserving lever — a lower-precision full head — recovers just **+38.3 TPS → ceiling 292.1 local (~302 official)**; even a *magically-free* head tops at **328.9 local, still −25% under the unsafe-class 442**. `quality_safe_ship_can_beat_442=FALSE`, `argmax_tax_is_dominant_gap_driver=TRUE`. So the head/precision lever is a **dead end for reaching the unsafe class** — the quality-safe ship is structurally ~292, because the head is only 82% of a 100-TPS gap and the remaining 18% is the body layers we cannot touch without the moat collapse. denken #540 (revived after the nudge) is the **deterministic** companion: per-position next-token TV between plain int4 base and the full fast-kernel stack is **exactly 0.0 across 41,962 positions, 0 argmax flips** — bit-identical, not "close" — closing the "kernel numerics tax" hypothesis fern's AIME wobble raised, at the bit level (10th convergent lens that fast-kernels + head-prune are quality-innocent, collapse is in the body-bake). The reseats test the **two remaining un-capped quality-safe speed levers**: denken #550 (is a *different* byte-identical kernel faster than the current fast stack? — orthogonal to precision, would *stack*) and lawine #551 (the KV-cache-read term #544's weight-decomp didn't cover). If both also cap <442, that's a robust multi-lever proof the quality-safe ship can't reach the unsafe class. **NO FIRE** — every cell `analysis_only`, `official_tps=0`, BASELINE 481.53 untouched.

## 2026-06-17 ~02:20Z — Cycle-57T: the AIME linchpin RESOLVES — fern #535 BANKED+MERGED (2f61dea); base_fullhead quality-safe to metric resolution on ALL FOUR axes; consolidated GO/NO-GO posted to Morgan #524 (NO FIRE — slow ship); reseat fern → #549 (cheap-candidate+full-verify, the quality-free head-speed lever)

**Theme — the chaotic axis that gated the whole base_fullhead quality verdict for cycles closes, and it closes the right way: at matched concurrency the fast kernels do NOT cost reasoning.** fern #535 reran AIME at **matched conc=32, n=90, greedy, identical item set**: base_fullhead **0.1444 (13/90) ≥ plain int4 base 0.1222 (11/90)**, ratio 1.18, diff(fh−base)=+0.022 (Newcombe95 [−0.079,+0.124] covers 0). The earlier "−0.10 AIME / 62.5% of base" read was **100% a concurrency confound** (fast@conc1 vs the n=30 conc=32 base=0.267 high draw) — now retired. Verdict vs the 0.90×base bar = **INCONCLUSIVE** (bar 0.110; point clears, Wilson-lo 0.086 doesn't) — underpowered because base itself is only 0.122 at matched conc, but **demonstrably NOT below base**. The first-token-EOS artifact is **ABSENT on AIME** (0/90 empties on all three draws; `min_tokens=8` a perfect no-op) — it's a GSM8K-prompt-distribution effect, not on the no-thinking greedy AIME path; as-served IS apples-to-apples. With this, the **base_fullhead quality triad is complete — no genuine reasoning loss on any of the four measured axes** (MMLU-Pro/GPQA 0.668/0.444 ubel #538; GSM8K served 0.850/96.8% wirbel #545; AIME 0.1444 fern #535). I posted the **promised consolidated #524 GO/NO-GO** (committed at 00:02Z to land "the moment the two gating cells land") — feasibility YES, quality clears to metric resolution, **TPS slow (253.78 = 71.7% of the unsafe ship) → two-gate UNMET → NO FIRE**. The reseat turns the binding constraint (speed of the quality-safe ship) into fern's next card: the full-precision 262k head is the single fattest **quality-free** TPS target (head-prune is innocent across 9 lenses; body-drop is the moat), and #549 asks whether a **two-stage cheap-candidate + full-verify** decode can capture it while keeping the greedy token byte-exact.

## 2026-06-17 ~01:55Z — Cycle-57S: the EOS-guard is a SERVED ship — wirbel #545 BANKED+MERGED (177e776), GSM8K base_fullhead promotes to served-by-default PASS (0.850, 96.8%) at ZERO cost; reseat wirbel → #548 (the osoi5-floor deconfound: is the SHIPPED ship secretly quality-safe?)

**Theme — the GSM8K leg of the base_fullhead triad closes end-to-end, and the cycle's reseat brackets the fast-AND-safe question from the opposite end.** wirbel #545 promotes the EOS-guard from "request-level recovered" (wirbel #541) to a **served default**: a one-line **chat-endpoint-scoped** `min_tokens=8` floor recovers GSM8K to **0.850 (96.8%, PASS)** with the served empty rate falling **10.4%→0.000**, and — the elegant part — it is **free by construction**. The floor lives only in `ChatCompletionRequest.to_sampling_params` (`/v1/chat/completions`, which GSM8K uses), while the TPS bench, the greedy-identity audit, and the teacher-forced PPL all run on the **byte-identical, source-unmodified `/v1/completions`** → PPL (2.006) and greedy token-identity *cannot* move; served TPS 252.6553 vs no-guard 252.4905 = **+0.16 (+0.065%, noise)**. base_fullhead is now quality-safe-confirmed on **two of three** short-chain axes (MMLU-Pro/GPQA via ubel #538; GSM8K served via #545); only AIME (fern #535) is still open. The reseat pivots wirbel to the one cell that could *short-circuit* the whole base_fullhead detour: **every osoi5 quality number on the board (MMLU-Pro 0.274 / GPQA 0.232 / AIME 0.033) was measured pre-EOS-discovery, `min_tokens=null`** — so #548 asks whether the SHIPPED 375.857 ship is secretly already quality-safe once its empties are floored (the complement of kanna #547's intact-body cell).

NO FIRE (`analysis_only`, `official_tps=0`).

### Reseat — wirbel → #548: is the SHIPPED osoi5 secretly quality-safe? (the osoi5-floor deconfound)
- **The unmeasured cell:** every osoi5 quality number (MMLU-Pro 0.274 / GPQA 0.232 / AIME 0.033) predates the EOS-artifact discovery — all measured `min_tokens=null`. A dropped/baked body is a *different, more damaged* EOS regime than base_fullhead; its actual served empty rate has never been measured. #548 serves the **as-shipped osoi5** (12k head-prune + baked body) and evaluates MMLU-Pro/GSM8K/GPQA **both as-served AND with the `min_tokens=8` floor**, per-arm empty rate. Deliverables: `osoi5_empty_rate_*`, `osoi5_recovers_with_floor`, `osoi5_floored_meets_gate`, `moat_is_genuine`, top-line `shipped_osoi5_is_secretly_quality_safe`.
- **Why it's the highest-value open measurement:** if osoi5+floor RECOVERS to the gate, the shipped **375.857** config is *already* fast-AND-quality-safe — an immediate #524 fire candidate (pending organizer re-open) that moots the entire slow-base_fullhead detour. The nine convergent lenses predict NO (body-bake owns the collapse; full recovery of 0.274→0.668 would need an implausible ~59% empty rate) — but none measured osoi5 served quality *with the floor*, so it's genuinely unmeasured. If it STILL COLLAPSES, the moat is genuine body-bake damage, hardened from the *fast* end. Pairs with kanna #547 (12k head on the INTACT body + floor) to decompose osoi5's collapse into {head-prune, body-bake, EOS-artifact}. LOCAL/`analysis_only`, NO FIRE, `--wandb_group osoi5-floor-deconfound`.

## 2026-06-17 ~01:25Z — Cycle-57R: the depth-prune lane is OPEN-BUT-NARROW (kanna #543, 12794334, 9th lens) + reseat kanna → #547 (the untested head×body corner: prune-head-on-INTACT-body)

**Theme — the body-drop speed lane is now formally closed, which sharpens the one remaining speed lever into focus.** kanna #543's offline minimum-damage drop-set census answers "is ANY layer-drop quality-safe?" with a precise **OPEN-BUT-NARROW**: a safe drop *exists* but only at trivial cardinality (k≤2, ~2.4–4.8% body FLOPs → a few TPS); every osoi5-scale (k≥5) drop-set FAILS the offline screen by ≥1.96×. **The cardinality that buys real TPS is exactly where quality dies.** This is the 9th independent lens on the head-innocent/body-owns-collapse finding, and it formally retires the "drop layers to go fast" idea. So the reseat pivots kanna to the **only untested cell of the head×body cube**: nine lenses say head-prune is *innocent*, but every one measured it as a *non-rescue of a broken body* (stark #536: full head on dropped body → 0.0% recovery) or with the *full* head on the intact body (ubel #538: 0.668/0.444). Nobody has measured **intact body × PRUNED head** — the cell that decides whether a FAST quality-safe ship exists (a narrow head erases lawine #544's dominant 262k-argmax tax AND re-enables the 16k-keyed MTP drafter → potentially ~300+ TPS at safe quality). kanna #547 measures it.

### kanna #543 — BANKED+MERGED (12794334) — offline minimum-damage drop-set census: depth-prune lane OPEN-BUT-NARROW (k≤2 only)
- kanna; run [`gqj4mb29`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/gqj4mb29) (group `bake-damage-locus`, finished); `analysis_only=true`, `official_tps=0`; **W&B-verified all 10 marker values, zero mismatches.** Raw-ablate (no heal), n=90, k1 census over all 42 layers + targeted k=2/k=5 sets; int4-floor = 0.0864 flip-rate (reproduces #539's 8.6%).

| drop-set | k | flip-rate | vs int4-floor 0.0864 | offline screen |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| int4-only (floor) | 0 | 0.0864 | 1.00× | — |
| **[37]** (min-damage) | **1** | **0.0557** | **0.64×** | **PASS** |
| [37,38] (best pair) | 2 | 0.0854 | 0.99× | PASS |
| mid-contig [34,35] | 2 | 0.1337 | 1.55× | FAIL |
| rd [3,36,37,38,39] | 5 | 0.1697 | 1.96× | FAIL |
| BI [33,36,37,38,39] | 5 | 0.1898 | 2.20× | FAIL |
| osoi5 [2,3,4,36,37] | 5 | 0.1911 | 2.21× | FAIL |
| mid-contig [31–35] | 5 | 0.2715 | 3.14× | FAIL (worst) |

- **Verdict:** `n_dropsets_passing=9` (8 singletons {2,3,4,35,36,37,38,39} + the one pair [37,38]); `any_quality_safe_drop_exists=true`; `depth_prune_lane_open="open-but-narrow-k<=2"`. The safe drops buy ~2.4–4.8% body FLOPs (marginal speed); the drops that buy real TPS (k≥5) all fail.
- **The #539 refinement (load-bearing):** #539 reported "Block-Influence *under-predicts* removal damage." #543 shows that was a **SET / cardinality effect, not single-layer mis-ranking.** As a *single-layer* predictor BI is GOOD — ρ=**0.761**, r=**0.812**, top-5 high-BI {23,0,1,22,41} overlaps 4/5 with the top-5 most-damaging singletons. BI is structurally blind to *composition*, so it fails the moment you stack k≥5. Both statements are true at their own cardinality; #543 reconciles them.
- **Three caveats bounding the hand-off:** (1) raw-ablate no-heal → a PASS is a *floor* on damage, NECESSARY-not-sufficient; (2) passers cluster in the heal-unreliable late-sharpening band (L36–39) — exactly where served+healed behavior is least predictable; (3) contiguity HURTS without heal (mid-contig [31–35] is the worst k=5 set). Only worth-serving drops: single late {36,37,38,39} or pair [37,38].
- **Relayed to ubel #546** (served depth Pareto): carve **[37,38] for drop=2**, **{36,37,38}/{37,38,39} for drop=3**, with the offline prior that **drop=3 likely fails** (cardinality cliff between k=2 and k=5). ubel's served+healed run is the binding measurement where the offline floor is least predictive.
- **Net:** hardens Morgan #524's structural read — a quality-safe *body* drop is real but structurally slow. No fire (`analysis_only`, `official_tps=0`; BASELINE 481.53 untouched).

## 2026-06-17 ~01:00Z — Cycle-57Q: the base_fullhead quality triad's GSM8K leg + served body 2×2 BANKED — wirbel #541 (92ac360, GSM8K 97.3% WITH a one-line EOS-guard; discovers a first-token-EOS *serving artifact*, not a reasoning loss) + ubel #538 (2ad4efe, served body 2×2: layer-removal dominates +0.208 vs int4 −0.012, base-int4 42L CLEARS the gate) → reseat wirbel → #545 (validate the EOS-guard as a SERVED ship) + ubel → #546 (served depth↔gate Pareto)

**Theme — base_fullhead is shaping up quality-safe on every measured short-chain axis, and the cycle's headline is a *recoverable serving artifact*.** Two triad/body cells landed and both point the same way. wirbel #541 measured the GSM8K leg and found the apparent FAIL (0.762, 86.8%) is **~10.4% immediate first-token-EOS empties** on the fast kernels over the 262k head — a *serving artifact, not reasoning loss* (the same items produce full correct CoT under sampling-escape + `enable_thinking`); a request-level `min_tokens=8` guard recovers 45/52 → **0.854 (97.3%, PASS) at full fast TPS 252**. ubel #538 measured the served body 2×2 and confirmed kanna #539's offline verdict from the opposite methodology: **layer-removal (not int4) owns the body collapse**, and **the genuine 42L base-int4 body — base_fullhead's exact body — CLEARS the gate (0.668/0.444)**. So MMLU-Pro/GPQA (body) clears; GSM8K clears with the guard; only AIME (fern #535) is still resolving. The reseats arm the two open follow-ups: (wirbel) make the EOS-guard a real *served* ship, and (ubel) find the depth↔gate Pareto knee.

### wirbel #541 — BANKED+MERGED (92ac360) — base_fullhead GSM8K leg + the first-token-EOS serving-artifact discovery (the cycle's unlock)
- wirbel; runs [`bfvbueb1`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/bfvbueb1) (gate table) / [`56qyjxm1`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/56qyjxm1) (TPS) / [`f4u9yf9z`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/f4u9yf9z) (decompose); `analysis_only=true`, `official_tps=0`; **W&B-verified** (guard_acc/empty_rate live in the decompose run f4u9yf9z and MATCH; regression_empty_share 0.5556 × 81 = 45.0 MATCH).

| GSM8K (n=500, seed=1234, 8-shot, sampled PRIMARY, conc=32) | acc | pct-of-base | gate (≥0.790) |
|---|---|---|---|
| base (vanilla gemma-4-E4B-it) | **0.878** | 100% | — |
| truefullhead (base-int4 262k + surgical fast kernels) **as-served** | 0.762 | 86.8% | **FAIL** |
| truefullhead **+ request-level `min_tokens=8`** | **0.854** | **97.3%** | **PASS** |

- **The discovery — a first-token-EOS serving artifact:** ~10.4% of completions are immediate `completion_tokens=1`, `content=''`, `finish_reason='stop'` empties on the fast kernels over the wide 262k head. **Not a reasoning loss** — the same items produce full correct CoT under sampling-escape + `enable_thinking`; the `min_tokens=8` floor recovers 45 of 52 empties. Genuine fast-kernel reasoning cost is only ≈−7pp (0.878→~0.85); the rest of the apparent shortfall was the artifact.
- **De-confound (osoi5 bake owns the real collapse):** the −68.2pp `base→osoi5-16k` is the bake (int4 re-quant + layer-removal + head bake), NOT the head width — truefullhead beats ship-12k **269-to-14** on the disagreement set. Consistent with the 8-lens body-owns-it finding.
- **TPS/PPL preserved:** the guard is a no-op on long benchmark generations → TPS **252.49** warm-median (peak 18.96 GiB), PPL **2.006** (fern #535) byte-exact unchanged.
- **The acute cross-axis risk (relayed):** an immediate-EOS zeros a MMLU-Pro/GPQA/AIME item exactly like a GSM8K item — AIME *harder* (long answers). So the artifact could hand a **false NO-GO** to stark #542 (MMLU/GPQA) and fern #535 (AIME), whose assignments predate this. Relayed to both ([#542](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/pull/542), [#535](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/pull/535)): report BOTH as-served AND with `min_tokens=8`, emit per-arm empty rate.
- **Scope (clean):** the eval-harness `--min-tokens` is an **opt-in request-level passthrough, default `None`** = byte-identical to prior; **no served-file change** (correctly flagged as a separate follow-up = wirbel's #545 reseat). The terminal marker reported the as-served 0.762 honestly (`pending_arms=false`); the guard arm is the apples-to-apples gate figure.

## 2026-06-17 ~00:40Z — Cycle-57P: TWO offline-mechanism cards BANKED — kanna #539 bake-damage locus (68a9463, layer-removal is the dominant body knob; BI under-predicts removal damage = 7th converging lens) + lawine #537 Medusa-1 (40e456f, NO-GO-LOCAL-REALIZE closes the last drafter family; Medusa 416 ≈ EAGLE-3 414, both <442) → reseat kanna → #543 (offline minimum-damage drop-set Pareto) + lawine → #544 (base_fullhead TPS-ceiling decomposition)

**Theme:** Two structural ceilings landed in one cycle, and together they harden the launch's central finding: **a quality-safe fast ship is structurally hard.** kanna #539 nails the body-damage mechanism — *layer-removal* (not int4 weight error) is the dominant knob, and the pruning heuristic that picked the removed layers (Block-Influence) under-predicts their removal cost. lawine #537 closes the *demand-side* speed search — Medusa-1, the last untried drafter family, ceilings at ~416 TPS, exactly where EAGLE-3 (#532) did (~414), both short of 442. So the two token-preserving speed levers — depth-prune and speculative drafting — are respectively *catastrophic to quality* and *capped below the unsafe class*. The reseats attack the two open quantitative questions this leaves: (kanna) is there ANY quality-safe drop-set at all, and (lawine) how fast CAN the quality-safe base_fullhead go once optimized.

### kanna #539 — BANKED+MERGED (68a9463) — bake-damage locus: layer-removal is the dominant body knob (7th converging lens)
- kanna; run [`hkrcys2w`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/hkrcys2w); `analysis_only=true`, `official_tps=0`; **W&B-verified all 10 numeric claims.** Offline teacher-forced divergence on an identical bf16 stream + an `ablate5` forward-hook counterfactual (removes the same 5 layers from clean base-bf16).

| signal | value | reading |
|---|---|---|
| `layerdrop_frac` / `int4_frac` | **0.664 / 0.336** | layer-removal owns ⅔ of the divergence; int4 weight error the other third |
| `posmatch_ablate5_also_flips_frac` | **0.8007** | 80.1% of osoi5's argmax flips reproduced by pure layer-removal alone |
| `damage_locus` | **CONCENTRATED** | localized to the dropped layers, not smeared across depth |
| osoi5 / int4 flip rate | **21.0% / 8.6%** | the bake ~doubles the int4-only flip rate; int4 final-layer masked cos 0.909 |
| `rebake_feasibility` | **cheap-targeted** | restore depth, NOT a full QAT retrain (banked as a FORECAST, see caveat) |

- **Headline finding — Block-Influence under-predicts removal damage:** the 5 removed layers {2,3,4,36,37} were the *lowest* BI in the model (36/37 at the 0th–2nd percentile) yet their removal is catastrophic. The depth-pruning selection rule was measuring the wrong thing — a transferable indictment of BI-guided pruning, not just this checkpoint.
- **7th converging lens — head-prune INNOCENT, body owns the collapse:** AIME Δprune=0.000 (#531) · GSM8K osoi5-16k≡ship-12k (#533) · offline coverage (#528) · MMLU-Pro+GPQA (#527) · per-position/bake-layer-85% (#529) · head-transplant recovers 0.0% (#536) · **per-layer offline locus (#539)**. Seven lenses, one verdict, zero dissent.
- **Decision impact:** explains the whole triad — base_fullhead keeps full 42L depth, which #539 says is the load-bearing property (restore depth → remove the dominant 0.66 damage knob). `rebake_feasibility=cheap-targeted` is banked as a *forecast, not a proven fix*, with the honest caveat front-and-center: **osoi5 already shipped a 37L heal that failed to recover the capability** — "cheap-targeted" names the lever, it doesn't promise the heal converges. Validity clean: `head_sanity 0.768 = keepset_coverage 0.934 × HF-vs-vLLM agreement 0.822`, both teacher-forced on the identical bf16 stream → neither confounds the split.

|---|---|---|

| acceptance ladder | [0.327, 0.180, 0.108, 0.081, 0.054] | …but independent heads decay fast → low E[T] cancels it |

- **Ceiling-vs-ceiling reconciliation with #532 (EAGLE-3) — the decisive cross-result:** two different demand-side drafter families, measured to their literature ceilings, converge — Medusa central-tree → **416 TPS**, EAGLE-3 → **414 TPS**, both ~415, both <442. Neither the autoregressive-feature drafter nor the parallel-head drafter clears the bar at the A10G per-step tax. **The drafter search space is now closed locally.** `best_ep=7` with the curve still improving → the binding constraint is **training data** (a cluster-scale corpus), not architecture.
- **Quality-neutral:** speculative decoding only changes E[T], never the emitted tokens (r1=r2=1.0 verify-identity) → purely a speed-ceiling result, costs nothing on the quality axis. Caveat banked: the `go_no_go="NO-GO-LOCAL-REALIZE"` string wasn't logged to summary, but the NO-GO is logically equivalent to the verified numerics (beats_442=0, crosses_500=0, projected 214, realized E[T] 1.655 < 3.42).

A head-route quality-safe ship would be slow AND need a *new* 262k-argmax kernel + 262k drafter that don't exist → not a lane.

The premise correction reorganizes the entire quality-fix strategy.** W&B [`vcacv804`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/vcacv804) (group `aime-substrate-prune-vs-bake`; verified independently: finished, `analysis_only=True`, `official_tps=0`, numbers match the comment cell-for-cell, base 0.267 & ship 0.033 reproduce the #514 greedy anchors). **Three-row decomposition (greedy maj@1, AIME-2024, n=30, seed=1234):** base (native 262k head) **0.267** → osoi5_full(=16k) **0.033** → ship(12k) **0.033**. `delta_prune(12k→16k)=0.000` (within ±0.093, CLEAN) · `delta_bake_confounded(base→16k)=+0.233` (outside ±0.174). **THE LOAD-BEARING PREMISE CORRECTION:** `LM_HEAD_PRUNE=0` does NOT yield a full 262k head — `osoi5-v0-baked/model.safetensors` is physically `lm_head=[16384,320]`; the **262k→16k prune is BAKED IN**, and the flag only does the second **16k→12k** drop (`serve.py:671`, verified on-pod from the bucket checkpoints + `pck04_keepset.json` keep_ids len 16384). So **every "full-head/no-prune via the flag" arm the fleet was building (ubel #527 / stark #530 / land #534 / wirbel #533) is a 16k substrate, not 262k** — and at 16k AIME is just as collapsed as 12k. `prune_is_the_cause=False`, `qat_bake_implicated=True`, `full_262k_head_osoi5_available=False`. The osoi5 BAKE (confounded with the bake-time 262k→16k prune + quant recipe), NOT the head width, holds the entire 0.233 AIME drop → **widening the keepset CANNOT rescue AIME (the steepest leg).** Extract-fail corroborates a real collapse (base 0.00 / osoi5-16k 0.37 / ship 0.30); greedy outputs of ship vs osoi5-16k diverge on 8/30 (the extra 4096 rows ARE sometimes argmax) yet neither lands a single extra correct → widening perturbs the trajectory without rescuing accuracy.

**Strategic consequence — the full-head fix path is CORRECTED, not abandoned.** "Quality-safe by construction" (no token `-inf`'d → base-identical head → collapse impossible) holds ONLY for a **genuine native 262k head**, which exists ONLY on **base-int4** (stock w4a16-ct, no osoi5 bake). A clean `delta_bake` (isolating the QAT bake from the 262k→16k prune) is ALSO obtainable only via that re-bake (fern follow-up #1, held).

- **Mechanism (localized):** the 12k head-prune, computed on a NARROW calibration set, `-inf`s the token the model wants on the broad MMLU/GPQA/AIME distribution → fluent reasoning, wrong final answer. The byte-exactness was always to the *357 substrate* (the pruned head), NOT to base — denken #520 / stark #509's "logit-identical to base" was measured on a no-prune composition / the calibration distribution, neither of which exercises the head-prune where it bites.

**★★ PR #520 (denken) — sampled-decoding quality-neutrality. BANKED — and it LOCALIZES the collapse, does not conflict with it.** W&B [`6wt6b5mk`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/6wt6b5mk) (re-pulled independently: finished, NaN-clean, 38/38 self-tests, every metric matches). The deployed int4 body (QKV/O/MLP + lm_head) routes through `apply_gptq_marlin_linear → torch.ops._C.marlin_gemm` — a **custom `_C` op outside aten** — so the flag **cannot reach it** (Marlin µs 465623 vs 459971, rel_diff 0.012, bit-identical). - **Conclusion:** **the strict frontier above the floor is reachable by running FEWER ops deterministically, not by finding a faster invariant GEMM.** A surgical attention-only pin keeps AOT compile + fast drafter + fast int4 body and pays only ~402 µs/cycle → ~457. Independently corroborates land #473's direct arm-(c) measurement. **Directly seeds the #481 reduction-sensitivity ladder** (reseated to ubel #491). 0-TPS attribution card → no BASELINE change. Student follow-up #2 (does the drafter even *need* batch-invariance for the greedy gate?) is subsumed by the #491 per-op census.

A genuinely fast strict rung requires **Δ_accept ≤ ~3.0%** (breach<5%) / ≤2.7% (breach<1%) — the drafter-hardening lane reseated to denken #492. Same Δ-decomposition + σ_hw anchors as #486/#379/#467, swept over TPS. SUPERSEDES #480's defunct-457 premise. 0-TPS analysis card → no BASELINE change.

(#407) — 🔴 REALIZED-NULL / ★ THE DRAFTER IS NOT TOO SMALL (D/V=0.222, 18.2% theoretical prize) — IT IS STRUCTURALLY UN-HIDEABLE BYTE-EXACT (land/async-pipelined-drafting): land profiled the served K=7 spec loop to test whether overlapping the drafter with verify (cycle → max(D,V)) lifts the realized frontier byte-exact. Verdict: **realized-NULL, but it fails for a MORE interesting reason than the self-abort gate anticipated.** Measured (decode-only steady-state, n≈280 steps, STEPTIME CUDA-event probe OUTSIDE the onegraph/loopgraph capture so the graphs are undisturbed): `drafter_D_ms_p50`=**1.433**, `verify_V_ms_p50`=**6.445**, `D_over_V_p50`=**0.2223** → the gate (D<0.05×V) does **NOT** trip (drafter is ~22% of verify, not <5%), so the *prize* is real — perfect overlap would cut the cycle by D/(D+V)=**18.2%**. **But `realized_async_delta_pct_byte_exact`=0.0 → `realized_async_tps_byte_exact`=467.14 (anchor unchanged).** **THE KILLER IS THE DATA DEPENDENCY, NOT THE MAGNITUDE:** the drafter's `propose(...)` consumes the prior pass's VERIFIED hidden state, so the critical path is strictly serial **V₍ₙ₋₁₎ → Dₙ → Vₙ** (Dₙ needs V₍ₙ₋₁₎'s verified state + the accept/reject decision of *which* position to read; Vₙ needs Dₙ's tokens). The same verified-state consumption that makes the drafter byte-exact (verify is the sole arbiter, land #420 `qe4qagc1`) is exactly what pins it to the serial path. **The PR's own prototype spec — drafter on stream-B with `stream_B.wait_event(verify_done)` — IS that serialization** (the wait reproduces the current D+V cycle with zero overlap): **byte-exact ⟺ wait_event ⟺ no overlap.** The only way to get overlap is to drop the wait → drafter consumes a not-yet-verified (contaminated) state → greedy identity breaks → fails the gate. **★ EVEN THE INADMISSIBLE CONTAMINATED PATH NETS ≤0 on a single bs=1 A10G** (3 independent confirmations): (1) the cited Saguaro paper ("Speculative Speculative Decoding", arXiv:2603.03251, ICLR 2026) does its byte-exact overlap by running the drafter on a **separate physical GPU** (1 draft + 4 verify GPUs, cross-device fan-out cache), never touching Vₙ's hidden state → single-GPU bs=1 inapplicable BY DESIGN; (2) a representative fp16 two-stream probe on THIS A10G (verify-like 7.02ms ∥ draft-like 1.60ms) → serial 8.62ms vs concurrent 7.91ms, overlap efficiency **0.44**, wall speedup only 8.3% (and that is the optimistic no-dependency, no-graph-cost case — int4-Marlin verify GEMMs saturate all 72 SMs so a concurrent drafter mostly queues); (3) disabling captured graphs to fork a stream costs **~1.0–1.5 ms/step** (CUDA graphs give ~3.1× on the MLP proposer) which EXCEEDS the ~0.6–0.7 ms/step a contaminated overlap could save → net ≤0. **★ RIGOR:** corrected the Saguaro citation to the multi-GPU mechanism (the single-GPU analog of why Saguaro needed a *separate* draft GPU); and made the right call NOT building the full prototype — the byte-exact `wait_event` spec is a provable no-op, so it would measure baseline-for-certain while requiring the flagged "disable the captured-graph fast path" risk for zero upside (measured D/V + structural proof + hardware probe is the stronger, lower-risk deliverable). Also self-measured the ubel #443 launch-overhead floor locally (graph-ON drafter dispatch 0.28 ms/step, graph-OFF +1–1.5 ms/step) since launch isolation barred inspecting #443 — moot here (byte-exact overlap saves 0 regardless of the floor). ppl 2.3772 (unchanged by construction — no served-path modification), self-abort gate not tripped (W&B [`0syyqxag`](https://wandb.ai/wandb-applied-ai-team/gemma-challenge-senpai/runs/0syyqxag) `async-pipelined-drafting`, FINISHED, NaN-clean, analysis_only/no_served_file_change=True official_tps=0, baseline 481.53 unchanged; advisor(8gpu)-merged 03:24:16Z [ec5586a], W&B INDEPENDENTLY VERIFIED — realized_async_tps_byte_exact=467.14 D_over_V_p50=0.2223 realized_async_delta_pct=0 self_abort_gate_trips=0 served_path_modified=False). **★ PROGRAM CONSEQUENCE (#407):** async-overlap joins cb3 (#437) and pinned-K (#433) as a **realized-NULL** — a modeled lever forfeiting to 0 on today's single-stream A10G kernel. The 5-card byte-exact decode-loop sweep is now **1 of 5 resolved (NULL)**; denken #441 (K-opt) / wirbel #442 (autotune) / ubel #443 (CUDA-graph) / stark #445 (KV-prefetch) remain in flight, and the fused build is already NO-GO at central (#440). **Reseated land #446** ([`drafter-retrain-demand-realize`](https://github.com/morganmcg1/gemma-challenge-senpai/pull/446)) — a time-boxed MTP drafter retrain to REALIZE the +15.36-TPS demand lift land itself sized in #439 (→ ≈482.5, the ONE identified path that beats the deployed 481.53 with equivalence; byte-exact-SAFE by construction since the verify arbiter makes a better drafter change accept-LENGTH, never emitted tokens).
